title: Customer Service Representative
description:
CUSTOMER SERVICE REPRESENTATIVE — MAGIC & FAMILY ENTERTAINMENT

Remote | Singapore Company | PHP 28,000 starting | PHP 33,000 after probation



At BirthdayMagicShow.sg & FamilyFunMagicShow.sg, we are one of Singapore’s leading party and event entertainment companies, specialising in highly interactive magic shows that are designed to entertain children and adults, young and old.



We create unforgettable magical experiences at birthday parties and family events all across Singapore - and we're looking for someone special to be the first point of contact for our clients.



If you love spreading joy, thrive on warm conversations, and know how to close a booking without ever sounding pushy - read on. 



==========================

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

==========================

• Starting salary: PHP 28,000/month

• After 3-month probation: PHP 33,000/month

• Commission opportunities unlocked after 6 months

• 14 days paid Annual Leave (after probation)

• 14 days paid Medical Leave (after probation)

• Philippines Public Holidays observed



==========================

WORK SCHEDULE

==========================

Working days: Monday, Tuesday, Friday, Saturday & Sunday

Hours: 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM

Setup: 100% remote / work from home



==========================

WHAT YOU'LL DO

==========================

• Be the first point of contact for families and clients booking our shows via WhatsApp — warm, prompt, and professional every time

• Understand each client's needs and match them to the right services and packages for their event

• Keep conversations moving toward a confirmed booking

• Guide customers through payment — smooth and stress-free

• Coordinate schedules between clients and our artists

• Follow up after events to collect reviews and show we care

• Support general admin and day-to-day operations

• Ensure our customers feel heard, happy, confident, and delighted about booking us



==========================

WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

==========================

You are who we are looking for if you are:



• Sales-driven yet genuine

You close bookings confidently without ever feeling pushy



• A natural over WhatsApp

Your tone is warm, clear, and professional in every message



• A self-starter

You take action without waiting to be told — and you own it



• Detail-oriented

 Multiple bookings, zero details falling through the cracks



• Resilient and upbeat

A tough conversation doesn't throw you - you treat customers with warmth, friendliness, and 5-star care



• In it for the long run

You are looking for a stable career in an understanding and family-oriented environment



==========================

REQUIREMENTS

==========================

• Excellent written and spoken English

• Proficient in Google Drive, Sheets, Docs, Calendar, WhatsApp, and ChatGPT

• Stable internet connection

• 2-monitor workstation setup (or more)

• Prior customer service experience is a plus - not a must



==========================

WHY JOIN US

==========================

• Family-oriented team - supportive and values-driven

• Understanding leadership that respects your work-life balance

• Full training and ongoing support provided to help you succeed in your role.

• Room to grow within the company

• A mission you'll actually enjoy showing up for every day



==========================

HOW TO APPLY

==========================

We review every application carefully. To be considered, please submit ALL four of the following:



1. YOUR RESUME / CV

   Highlight any experience in customer service, sales, or admin.



2. A 2-MINUTE LOOM VIDEO

   Record at 
 - introduce yourself and tell us why you're a great fit. Be yourself. This is how we get to know you.



3. YOUR 16PERSONALITIES RESULT

   Take the free test at 
 and send us the link to your results.



4. A SPEEDTEST SCREENSHOT

   Run a test at speedtest.net and send us a screenshot.



! Only applications using 10 or more Apply Points (APs) will be considered.

! Applications missing any of the 4 requirements above will not be reviewed. 

! Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

! Deadline: 27 March 2026



==========================

ABOUT US

==========================

At BirthdayMagicShow.sg and FamilyFunMagicShow.sg, we spread joy and wonder to events across Singapore. Our team of magicians, balloon artists, caricaturists, face painters, and hand painters create memories that families and clients talk about for years.



We're a lean and close-knit company, and genuinely proud of our mission to spread joy~



We look forward to having you onboard!

title: Course Creator / LMS Specialist for Real Estate Training Programs
description:
Job Description:

We are looking for an experienced course creator to build engaging online training courses for our real estate business. This role involves creating both real estate agent training courses and SOP/staff training courses inside TalentLMS or LearnWorlds.



The ideal candidate has hands-on experience developing professional online learning content, editing videos, embedding YouTube videos, organizing course modules, and adding quizzes and other interactive elements. Experience working on real estate-related training is strongly preferred.



Responsibilities:



Build online training courses from provided content, outlines, or source materials



Create clear, professional course structures for agent training and staff SOP training



Edit and format video content for online learning



Embed YouTube videos and other multimedia into courses



Add quizzes, assessments, and interactive learning components



Upload and organize content within TalentLMS or LearnWorlds



Ensure courses are user-friendly, visually polished, and easy to navigate



Recommend best practices for learner engagement and course completion



Required Qualifications:



Mandatory experience with TalentLMS or LearnWorlds



Experience creating and launching online courses from start to finish



Strong video editing skills



Experience embedding videos, creating quizzes, and formatting LMS content



Ability to take raw content and turn it into a polished training experience



Strong attention to detail and organized project management



Preferred Qualifications:



Experience creating real estate training courses



Familiarity with real estate agent onboarding, compliance, sales, or operations training



Experience developing SOP-based staff training programs



Instructional design experience is a plus



Ideal Candidate:

You are creative, organized, and experienced in turning business knowledge into engaging online learning. You understand how to structure lessons so they are easy to follow, professional, and effective for both new and existing tea
mbers.



To Apply:

Please send:



Examples of courses you have built



Which LMS platforms you have used (TalentLMS and/or LearnWorlds)



Details of any real estate-related course work



Your rate and estimated turnaround time

ing, and hopping on calls

title: Creative Operations VA (UGC & Viral Content) — High-Performance Role
description:
We are looking for a top-tier Creative Operations VA to manage daily content planning and client communication across dozens of campaigns.



This is not a basic assistant role. This position directly drives client results and campaign success. You will be the central hub between clients, creators, editors, and schedulers.



If you are highly organized, fast-thinking, culturally fluent in the U.S. market, and deeply experienced in UGC and viral short-form content, we want to hear from you.



What You’ll Be Responsible For



You will manage communication and execution for 50+ client campaigns across multiple platforms.



Daily responsibilities include:



Monitoring and responding across Slack, WhatsApp, 
, and project tools



Understanding each client’s goals, brand voice, and campaign requirements



Writing daily content plans for creators



Creating scripts, hooks, captions, and shot lists



Assigning tasks clearly in a to-do format



Researching competitor content and campaign examples for inspiration



Reviewing what other creators on the campaign are producing



Organizing raw files for editors and schedulers



Ensuring content flows smoothly from concept ? creation ? posting



Tracking deliverables and deadlines across many moving parts



Some clients provide detailed instructions. Others do not. You must be able to extract clarity from chaos and move projects forward without hand-holding.



Content Expertise Required



You must deeply understand short-form content formats, including:



Talking-head videos



Long text hooks



Skits and story-based content



Reaction videos



High-volume organic UGC



TikTok / Reels / Shorts best practices



Viral content structures and retention tactics



You should know how to craft content that feels native, authentic, and engaging for U.S. audiences.



Who This Role Is For



You are:



Extremely organized and detail-oriented



Fast, decisive, and proactive



Fluent in written English at a near-native level



Culturally aligned with U.S. social media trends



Comfortable managing many conversations at once



Able to prioritize under pressure



Resourceful when instructions are incomplete



Obsessed with execution and outcomes



Bonus if you have worked in agencies, creator management, or high-volume content teams.



Who This Role Is NOT For



This is not a beginner position.



Do not apply if you:



Need constant direction



Struggle with time management



Are unfamiliar with UGC or short-form content



Cannot handle fast-paced environments



Prefer routine, predictable tasks



Hours:

This is a US hours job you will have to work EST potential to shift to standard Philippines hours after training

title: Website, SEO, Google Business Profile & Digital/Phone Marketing
description:
We run a growing home care business that helps seniors live longer, healthier, and more independent lives in their own homes. We’re focused on results, sustainability, and long-term growth—not hype, vanity metrics, or shortcuts.



We’re looking for a skilled Virtual Assistant who understands how websites, SEO, Google Business Profiles, and marketing systems work together to consistently generate qualified inquiries and phone calls.



This is a hands-on execution role, not a strategy-only role.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

Website Management



Maintain and update our website (pages, content, basic UX improvements)



Ensure site speed, mobile optimization, and basic technical SEO hygiene



Implement landing pages or service pages that support local lead generation



SEO (Local & On-Page)



On-page SEO (titles, meta descriptions, headers, internal linking)



Local SEO focused on service areas



Keyword research with intent (calls & inquiries, not traffic for traffic’s sake)



Monitor rankings and make adjustments based on performance



Google Business Profile (GBP)



Optimize and manage Google Business Profile(s)



Weekly posts, photos, and updates



Review monitoring and response support



Track GBP insights and actions (calls, direction requests, clicks)



Digital & Phone Call Marketing



Support digital campaigns designed to generate phone calls, not just clicks



Assist with call tracking setup and basic reporting



Help optimize ads, landing pages, and messaging based on call quality



Coordinate simple follow-ups or workflows tied to inbound calls



Reporting & Accountability



Weekly reporting on:



Website performance



SEO progress



Google Business activity



Calls and inquiries generated



Flag what’s working, what’s not, and what needs fixing



What We’re Looking For



Required Skills & Experience



Proven experience managing:



Websites (WordPress or similar)



Local SEO



Google Business Profiles



Solid understanding of call-driven local marketing



Comfortable using tools like:



Google Search Console



Google Analytics



Call tracking tools



SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, or similar)



Strong attention to detail and follow-through



You’re a Good Fit If You:



Care more about results than buzzwords



Can execute without constant hand-holding



Think in systems, not random tasks



Are comfortable being measured on outcomes



Communicate clearly and honestly when something isn’t working



What This Is NOT



Not a “post on social media and hope” role



Not a branding or influencer role



Not a place for shortcuts, spammy SEO, or fake metrics



Work Details



Remote / Virtual



Part-time to start (with potential to grow)



Flexible hours, outcome-focused



Long-term opportunity for the right person



How to Apply



Please include:



A brief summary of your relevant experience



Examples of websites, SEO work, or Google Business Profiles you’ve managed



One short paragraph explaining how you help local businesses get more phone calls



Finally if you got this far, please add on the title line Gemini for me to get your attention.

title: Marketing and SEO coordinator
description: We run a growing home care business that helps seniors live longer, healthier, and more independent lives in their own homes. Our focus is on real results, sustainability, and long-term growth—not hype, vanity metrics, or shortcuts. We’re looking for a skilled Marketing and SEO Coordinator (Virtual Assistant) who understands how websites, SEO, Google Business Profiles, and marketing systems work together to consistently generate qualified inquiries and phone calls. This is a hands-on execution role, not a strategy-only role. You’ll be responsible for maintaining and improving our website (including content updates, basic UX improvements, site speed, mobile optimization, and technical SEO hygiene), building and optimizing service and landing pages for local lead generation, and executing on-page and local SEO including keyword research focused on intent (calls and inquiries, not empty traffic), internal linking, and performance tracking. You’ll also manage and optimize our Google Business Profile(s) through regular posts, photos, updates, review monitoring and responses, and tracking key actions like calls, clicks, and direction requests. On the marketing side, you’ll support and execute digital campaigns designed to generate phone calls, assist with call tracking setup and reporting, help improve ads, landing pages, and messaging based on call quality, and coordinate simple follow-ups tied to inbound calls. You’ll provide weekly reporting on website performance, SEO progress, Google Business activity, and calls and inquiries generated, clearly identifying what’s working, what’s not, and what needs to be fixed. We’re looking for someone with proven experience managing websites (WordPress or similar), local SEO, and Google Business Profiles, who understands call-driven local marketing and is comfortable using tools like Google Search Console, Google Analytics, call tracking software, and SEO platforms such as Ahrefs or SEMrush. You should have strong attention to detail, follow-through, and the ability to execute without constant hand-holding, think in systems rather than random tasks, and be comfortable being measured on outcomes. This is not a social media posting role, not a branding or influencer position, and not a place for shortcuts, spammy SEO, or fake metrics. This is a remote, part-time role to start with flexible hours and a clear path to grow into a long-term position for the right person. To apply, include a brief summary of your relevant experience, examples of websites, SEO work, or Google Business Profiles you’ve managed, and one short paragraph explaining how you help local businesses generate more phone calls. Finally, if you’ve read this far, add the word Saturn to the title line so we know you’re paying attention.

title: Technical Writer
description:
Description

The Technical Writer will develop and maintain safety-focused documentation that supports our clients and internal stakeholders. This role will be instrumental in writing, editing, and refreshing materials such as safety manuals, documentation, and compliance content. The ideal candidate thrives on accountability, consistently meets deadlines, and contributes to clear and compliant safety communications.



Key Responsibilities:

- Write, edit, and format safety manuals, policies, procedures, and guidance documents.

- Refresh and revise existing documents to ensure accuracy and compliance with current OSHA/HSE standards.

- Draft materials for construction site safety meetings and toolbox talks.

- Prepare clear, concise summaries and follow-ups from safety meetings and client engagements.

- Incorporate regulatory requirements and best practices into content by researching OSHA and industry standards.

- Ensure all documents are delivered with exceptional attention to detail, accurate technical information, consistent terminology, and proper formatting.

- Collaborate with subject-matter experts to validate technical content.

- Prioritize and manage multiple projects concurrently while meeting deadlines.

- Track deliverables to ensure timely completion with minimal revision cycles.



Requirements

- Proven experience as a Technical Writer, preferably with safety, compliance, or industrial content.

- Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.

- strong attention to detail and ability to produce error-free documentation.

- High level of accountability and demonstrated ability to reliably meet deadlines.

- Comfortable multitasking and managing shifting priorities.



Preferred

- Bilingual in Spanish and English

- OSHA 30-Hour Training Certification or equivalent safety training experience.

- Experience writing safety manuals, compliance documentation, or construction-focused materials.

- Familiarity with contractor compliance platforms and requirements.

- Experience working with subject matter experts and translating technical language into clear, user-friendly content.



Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:



Internet Connection:

Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.

Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.

Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.

Primary Device:

Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:

Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.

A minimum of 8 GB RAM.

Backup Device:

Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.

Must be functional during power interruptions.

A functioning webcam.

A noise-canceling USB headset.

A quiet, dedicated home office space.

Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.

title: HubSpot Coordinator
description:
Description

Overview: This role focuses on driving the empowerment of the Sales Team and optimizing sales processes for increased productivity. The ideal candidate will be proficient in leveraging cutting-edge tools like HubSpot, Apollo, and Zapier to streamline operations and boost organizational efficiency. Expertise in enhancing CRM databases for effective Business Development Representative (BDR) outreach, while ensuring data accuracy and system optimization, is essential. A proven track record of streamlining sales processes and improving sales outcomes is a must.



Main Responsibilities:



Lead Enrichment & Data Management: Manage and optimize lead enrichment processes, including data management, system analysis, and ongoing lead quality checks.

HubSpot CRM Management: Oversee CRM management, including assisting with lead assignment and reassignment to the sales team, ensuring all records are up-to-date and relevant.

Lead Enrichment Research: Use Apollo and LinkedIn to research and enrich leads with relevant information to increase outreach success.

Data Quality & Cleanup: Monitor and maintain data quality, deduplicate records, and address inconsistencies in the CRM.

Reporting & Dashboards: Develop and maintain performance reports and dashboards to track key metrics and provide insights for the sales and marketing teams.

Tool Integration & Reporting: Integrate with sales tools such as RingCentral to ensure in-sync, accurate reporting across platforms.

Lead Scrubbing & Accuracy: Proactively crawl new leads and scrub them for accuracy and relevance.

Workflow Optimization: Identify inefficiencies in current HubSpot workflows, recommend, and implement process improvements.

Support & Ad-Hoc Analysis: Provide support for ad-hoc data analysis requests, task creation for BDRs, and lead follow-ups.

Cross-Department Collaboration: Work closely with sales, marketing, and operations teams to streamline communications and data handoffs.

Minor Responsibilities:



Facilitation & Communication: Participate in calibration sessions and meetings to ensure clear and effective communication across departments.

BDR Support: Maintain open communication with BDRs to address inquiries and provide ongoing support for lead-related activities.

Team Training & Development: Help train new tea
mbers, providing continuous guidance and support to ensure their long-term success.

KPI & Reporting: Report on KPIs, tools, and overall department performance to key stakeholders.

Requirements

Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Business Management, Entrepreneurship, or other related fields.

Required Work Experience:



1-2 years of experience in lead generation, data management, or B2B sales.

At least 6 months of experience with CRM platforms, ideally HubSpot.

1-2 years in client success or end-to-end sales experience is preferred.

Hands-on experience with Zapier integration for sales workflows and automation.

Hard Skills:



Data Management: Proficiency in organizing and maintaining data.

System Analysis: Ability to analyze and optimize systems to improve lead enrichment and management processes.

CRM Management: Expertise in CRM management, particularly with HubSpot.

Lead Enrichment: Experience with enriching leads using tools like Apollo and LinkedIn.

Data Quality Assurance: Proactive approach to scrubbing and ensuring the accuracy of leads and CRM data.

Research Skills: Strong ability to gather and interpret relevant information for lead enrichment.

Analytical Skills: Ability to analyze data and draw actionable insights for decision-making.

Zapier Integration: Proficiency in using Zapier for automating workflows and integrating tools to improve team efficiency.

Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:



Internet Connection:

Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.

Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.

Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.

Primary Device:

Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:

Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.

A minimum of 8 GB RAM.



Backup Device:

Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.

Must be functional during power interruptions.

A functioning webcam.

A noise-canceling USB headset.

A quiet, dedicated home office space.

Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.

title: Remote Real Estate Mentor & Agent Accountability Coordinator
description:
Summary

Location: Remote

Reports To: Broker of Record

Employment Type: Full-Time Remote



EXPERIENCED APPLICANTS ONLY

Must Be Fluent in DotLoop,Zillow, Lofty/Chime CRM, and real estate lead management systems



Position Summary

We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and coach-minded Remote Real Estate Mentor & Agent Accountability Coordinator  to support our brokerage’s agents through consistent communication, performance tracking, and lead accountability.



This person will work closely with all agents in the brokerage, helping ensure they stay engaged with leads, complete training deliverables, follow up consistently, and maintain high standards inside Zillow and Lofty. This role is a mix of mentor, coach, scorekeeper, and accountability partner. The right candidate will help agents stay honest, on track, and productive by conducting regular check-ins, monitoring follow-up activity, reviewing lead conversion metrics, and reporting performance trends to leadership.



This is a remote support role that plays a critical part in agent development, lead management, and brokerage growth.



Key Responsibilities

Agent Communication & Check-Ins

• Maintain regular contact with every agent in the brokerage.

• Conduct scheduled check-ins with agents regarding lead follow-up, pipeline activity, training progress, and production goals.

• Hold agents accountable for completing required lead activities, follow-up tasks, and academy assignments.

• Document agent feedback, blockers, action items, and next steps after each check-in.

• Escalate repeated lack of follow-up, poor responsiveness, or missed deliverables to leadership.

Training Academy Support

• Support the brokerage’s training academy by tracking attendance, participation, and completion of required assignments.

• Reinforce training topics with agents through one-on-one follow-up and coaching.

• Ensure agents complete required deliverables tied to training, lead response, prospecting, and conversion.

• Identify agents who need extra support and coordinate follow-up coaching.



Zillow Lead Scorecard Management

• Maintain and update a Zillow lead scorecard for all agents.

• Track lead response times, contact attempts, appointment setting, nurtures, active conversations, and conversion outcomes.

• Monitor each agent’s lead conversion rate and compare performance over time.

• Ensure lead notes, follow-up status, and dispositions are properly entered and updated.

• Provide weekly agent-level and brokerage-level performance summaries.



Lofty & Zillow System Oversight

• Review agent activity inside Lofty and Zillow to ensure leads are being worked properly.

• Audit lead routing, notes, statuses, drip campaigns, tasks, and pipeline hygiene.

• Identify missed leads, stale leads, overdue follow-ups, incomplete notes, and poor CRM usage.

• Recommend improvements to workflows, follow-up systems, and accountability practices.

• Help maintain clean data and consistent standards across both platforms.



Performance Monitoring & Reporting

• Track KPIs related to lead management, appointments, conversions, responsiveness, and agent compliance.

• Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports for leadership.

• Highlight wins, coaching opportunities, and risk areas.

• Provide constructive feedback to agents on follow-up quality, consistency, and conversion habits.

• Support leadership in improving agent accountability and brokerage performance.



Core Duties

• Check every agent’s lead activity daily.

• Follow up with agents who have overdue or untouched leads.

• Collect lead updates and required deliverables from agents.

• Review CRM notes and verify follow-up actions are complete.

• Track conversion metrics and identify trends.

• Support the onboarding and development of newer agents.

• Maintain accurate records of agent accountability conversations.

• Assist in building a culture of ownership, responsiveness, and consistency.



Qualifications

• Previous experience in real estate operations, ISA support, transaction coordination, sales support, coaching coordination, or agent success preferred.

• Familiarity with Zillow, Lofty CRM, and real estate lead management systems strongly preferred.

• Strong written and verbal English communication skills.

• High level of organization, follow-through, and attention to detail.

• Comfortable holding agents accountable in a professional and respectful way.

• Ability to track data, maintain reports, and identify performance patterns.

• Strong sense of urgency and time management.

• Proficient in spreadsheets, reporting, and CRM systems.

• Able to work independently in a remote environment.



Preferred Traits

• Coachable, assertive, and professional

• Detail-oriented and process-driven

• Supportive but firm with accountability

• Analytical and comfortable with numbers

• Consistent, dependable, and discreet

• Positive attitude with a service mindset

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

• Percentage of agents contacted weekly

• Check-in completion rate

• Lead scorecard accuracy and timeliness

• Zillow lead response compliance

• Agent follow-up compliance rate

• Lead conversion rate by agent

• Number of overdue/stale leads corrected

• Academy deliverable completion rate

• CRM note completion and data hygiene score

• Weekly leadership reporting completion



Success in This Role Looks Like

• Every agent is being contacted consistently

• No lead is left untouched or unmanaged

• Zillow and Lofty are clean, current, and actionable

• Agents are submitting updates and completing deliverables

• Leadership has clear visibility into performance and accountability

• Agents improve in consistency, responsiveness, and conversion

title: Operations Manager (Solar Operations)
description:
ABOUT SUNSENT

SunSent is a fast-growing U.S.-based solar and roofing company. We are expanding our operations and looking for a highly organized, process-driven Operations Manager to lead and optimize our solar operations.



We are looking for someone structured, detail-oriented, and execution-focused, someone who can build systems, manage teams, and ensure smooth coordination across departments.



COMPENSATION

• $350 – $400/month (depending on experience)

• Paid weekly



ROLE OVERVIEW

As our Operations Manager, you will:

• Oversee day-to-day solar operations from sale to installation

• Manage workflow across sales, permitting, scheduling, and installation teams

• Build and improve operational systems and processes (SOPs)

• Track project timelines, backlog, and completion rates

• Ensure clear communication between departments

• Identify bottlenecks and implement solutions

• Maintain accurate reporting and performance tracking

• Coordinate closely with U.S.-based leadership



You will report directly to our COO.



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Proven experience in operations management (solar, construction, or service-based industry preferred)

• Strong understanding of workflow management and process improvement

• Experience managing teams and holding accountability

• Excellent organizational and problem-solving skills

• Strong communication skills (written and verbal)

• Experience using CRM and operations tools such as AccuLynx, Dialpad, and similar platforms

• Highly structured, disciplined, and detail-oriented

• Must be comfortable working U.S. business hours (Central Standard Time)



NICE TO HAVE

• Experience in solar operations (permitting, installation, interconnection)

• Familiarity with EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System)

• Experience working with U.S.-based teams



WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are specifically looking for candidates who are:

• Detail-oriented

• Process-driven and highly structured

• Strong in execution and accountability

• Data-driven and analytical

• Proactive problem-solvers

• Strong leaders who can manage and align teams



This is a highly structured, performance-driven role where consistency, organization, and follow-through are critical to success.



(We utilize Culture Index as part of our hiring process.)



HOW TO APPLY

To apply, please submit:

• Your updated resume

• A short message explaining your operations management experience

• Any relevant experience in solar or roofing operations



Only shortlisted candidates will move to the next stage.



We’re excited to meet a strong Operations Manager who can help scale and strengthen SunSent’s operations.



Apply now!

title: CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE COORDINATOR (FAMILY EXPERIENCE TEAM | ATTIO PREFERRED)
description:
ABOUT RELATIONAL MINDS



Relational Minds is an Australian child and family mental health organisation delivering relationship-focused, evidence-informed programs to parents, children, educators, and leaders. Our philosophy is grounded in relationship-first approaches, compassionate communication, and trauma-informed, evidence-based practice.



THE OPERATIONAL CONTEXT



We coordinate complex assessment and treatment pathways involving multiple clinicians and strict timelines. Our Family Experience Team ensures every family’s journey is organised, visible, and followed through.



We are transitioning from spreadsheet-based tracking to a modern relationship management system using Attio CRM. Attio will serve as our central system for managing family journeys, lifecycle stages, and service coordination.



WHY WORK WITH US

	•	Values: We value kindness, clarity, and reliable teamwork.

	•	Culture: We offer a calm, respectful, emotionally intelligent work environment.

	•	Leadership: You will work with supportive Australian leadership that values initiative and good judgement.

	•	Growth: This is a meaningful long-term role where you will help shape how our systems evolve as the organisation grows.



THE ROLE



You will coordinate the end-to-end journey for families, acting as the operational guide who ensures their progress through our service stays organised and on schedule.



This is a coordination role requiring assertive communication, structured thinking, and strong system management.



You will work primarily within Attio CRM, helping maintain a clear view of where each family sits in their journey, ensuring tasks are completed on time, and identifying risks before delays occur.



You will work within a small Customer Experience team and report to the Customer Experience Manager.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

	•	Manage the family journey

Track each family’s progress from intake through assessment, treatment blocks, and follow-up using Attio pipelines and lifecycle stages.

	•	Maintain data integrity

Ensure Attio remains the central “source of truth” for family records, service stages, and communication history.

	•	Coordinate timelines

Monitor clinician and administrative tasks and follow up when deadlines are approaching or overdue.

	•	System management

Maintain clean, structured CRM records including custom fields, tags, and views that allow the team to quickly see where attention is needed.

	•	Risk identification

Identify delays, missing information, or stalled cases and escalate to the Customer Experience Manager.

	•	Documentation coordination

Ensure consents, reports, and key documents are correctly linked and stored within our Microsoft 365 environment.



REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

	•	Strong CRM experience

Experience using a CRM to manage customer or client journeys across multiple stages.

	•	Attio CRM experience strongly preferred

Familiarity with Attio pipelines, custom fields, views, and workflow organisation.

	•	Structured data management

Experience maintaining accurate records and ensuring systems remain clean and reliable.

	•	Workflow coordination

Proven ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders and follow up professionals to ensure timelines are met.

	•	Professional written communication

Strong written English and the ability to communicate calmly with Australian families.

	•	Experience working with Australian companies

Understanding Australian professional expectations and communication style.



HIGHLY REGARDED

	•	Experience in Australian healthcare, allied health, or NDIS-related services

	•	Experience implementing or managing CRM systems during organisational growth

	•	Familiarity with automation tools such as Zapier, Power Automate, or CRM workflow automation

	•	Background in operations coordination, client success, or service delivery



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

	•	Not a general Virtual Assistant role

	•	Not a passive data-entry position

	•	Not a call centre role



You will actively coordinate workflows and ensure families and clinicians stay on track.



WORK DETAILS

	•	Remote position (work from home)

	•	Full-time, long-term role

	•	Work aligned with Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) business hours

	•	Direct collaboration with an Australian leadership team



HOW TO APPLY



Please include in your application:

	•	A summary of your experience using CRM systems to track customer or client journeys

	•	Your experience using Attio CRM or similar platforms

	•	Your experience working with Australian companies

	•	One example of how you followed up with professionals to keep a workflow on schedule



Shortlisted candidates will complete a short practical task demonstrating how they would structure and manage a client journey within a CRM system.

title: Revenue Cycle & Operations Coordinator
description:
Job Title: Revenue Cycle & Operations Coordinator (Part-Time)

Hours: Up to 15 hours per week

Location: Remote / Southern California

Reports To: CEO / Practice Leadership



About Calyx

Calyx Practice Solutions is a growing healthcare organization focused on delivering high-quality patient care while building strong operational systems that support long-term growth. Our team works closely with patients, healthcare providers, and partner agencies to ensure care delivery, communication, and administrative processes run smoothly.



Position Overview

The Revenue Cycle & Operations Coordinator is responsible for helping ensure the organization is paid accurately and on time for the care it provides. This role oversees key parts of the insurance claims process, including claim corrections, denial resolution, payer communication, and reimbursement tracking.

This is a part-time role (up to 15 hours per week) designed to provide consistent oversight of the growing  organization’s revenue cycle while also supporting operational reporting and coordination.

The position works closely with providers, clinical staff, front office staff, and leadership to help ensure billing processes and operational workflows function efficiently.



Key Responsibilities

Revenue Cycle Management

Review and monitor insurance claims in the EHR system

Investigate and resolve denied or rejected claims

Submit corrected claims or appeals when necessary

Communicate with insurance payers regarding claim status

Track reimbursements and unpaid claims

Reporting and Tracking

Maintain spreadsheets tracking claim status and reimbursements

Identify patterns in claim denials or billing issues

Support simple operational reporting for leadership

Operational Coordination

Work with front office and clinical staff to resolve documentation or insurance issues affecting billing

Support improvements to billing and operational workflows

Assist with credentialing documentation or payer communication when needed



Qualifications

Required

Experience in healthcare administration, medical billing, or revenue cycle management

Experience with Medicare is a must

Strong working knowledge of Excel or Google Sheets

Strong attention to detail and organization

Ability to manage tasks independently and follow issues through resolution

Preferred

Experience with Athenahealth or another EHR system

Familiarity with healthcare billing workflows and denial management

Experience with Tableau or other data visualization tools



Why This Role Matters

Revenue cycle performance directly affects the financial health of a healthcare practice. This role helps ensure claims are processed correctly, reimbursements are received in a timely manner, and operational systems remain organized as the organization grows.

title: ELITE VIDEO EDITOR - ALL FORMATS
description:
We are not looking for someone who is good at editing. We are looking for someone who is exceptional at it and cannot stop getting better. If you need direction, heavy notes, or hand-holding, this is not the role. If you independently raise the bar on every single cut, keep reading.

About DRAX Social



DRAX Social is a full-service social media video production agency. We create content that performs for brands, creators, and businesses across every major platform. Our team moves fast, sets the bar high, and does not slow down for average work.



What This Role Actually Is: 

This is a multi-format editor role. You will edit across every style and format we produce. Podcasts are one slice. The rest is just as important.

- Short-form social content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) across multiple brand voices and aesthetics

- Long-form YouTube content: talking head, educational, vlog, documentary-style

- Podcast episodes and high-performing podcast clip packages

- Music videos and festival / event recap content with strong visual rhythm

- Brand and product content: clean, polished, intentional

- Vertical trailers, teasers, and promotional cuts



You do not need to be equally strong in all of these on day one. But you need to be genuinely excellent in at least 3, and actively working on the rest. Style-switching is not optional here. It is the job.



What You Will Actually Do:

- Edit long-form content with exceptional pacing, clarity, and polish. No bloat, no dead air, no sloppy cuts.

- Build high-performing short-form clips from raw footage and longer content, independently - identifying the best hooks and moments.

- Adapt your editing style across formats without losing quality or needing creative direction each time.

- Apply captions, graphics, sound design, and pacing choices appropriate to each specific format and platform.

- Deliver edits that are essentially revision-ready, not first drafts that need heavy notes.

- Use AI tools strategically to improve speed, discoverability, and output quality.

- Own your projects from receipt of raw footage to final delivery.

- Communicate clearly, proactively, and professionally. No chasing, no guessing.



What We Require - NON NEGOTIABLES

- Expert-level Adobe Premiere Pro. Not intermediate, not learning. You know the shortcuts, the workarounds, the fast paths.

- Strong working knowledge of After Effects for motion, titles, and custom graphics.

- Demonstrated experience editing content that actually performed well. Clips that went viral, formats that drove results.

- A genuine ear for pacing, rhythm, and storytelling across wildly different tones.

- Detail obsession. You notice the audio hiss, the misaligned caption, the half-frame hold. And you fix it before anyone asks.

- Fluent English. You understand nuance, tone, and conversational flow without needing it explained.

- Comfort working overnight PST hours (graveyard shift) on a consistent schedule.

- Reliability that is non-negotiable. Deadlines are not suggestions here.



Who This Is NOT For

- People who need a checklist to know what a good edit looks like.

- Editors who go quiet when things get busy or ambiguous.

- People who submit drafts expecting feedback to do the creative heavy lifting.

- Editors who are one-format specialists and uninterested in expanding.

- Anyone whose best work is 3 years behind them.



What We Offer:

- Fully remote. Work from wherever. Operate on PST hours.

- Consistent, high-output work. Not slow drip freelance.

- Real variety. You will touch multiple brands, formats, and styles constantly.

- A team that actually cares about the quality of the work and holds a genuine standard.



How to Apply:

We read every application. We skip every one that is incomplete. Include all of the following or do not apply.

- 3 links to short-form clips you edited that performed well (Reels, TikTok, or Shorts). Tell us the view count or reach if you know it.

- 1 link to a long-form piece you edited: podcast, YouTube, documentary, interview. Any format. Must be over 20 minutes.

- 1 link to content you edited in a style completely different from the above. Music, festival recap, brand spot, event, anything that shows range.

- A short note (3 to 5 sentences) on what separates an average editor from a great one. We want to hear how you think, not your resume summary.

- The name of a piece of content (video, podcast, show, anything) that you think is edited brilliantly. Tell us one specific reason why.



Applications that are missing any of the above, use a generic cover letter, or appear to be mass-submitted will not receive a response.

title: Operations & Client Experience Manager
description:
About the Company

My company is a boutique psychotherapy practice in New York City designed for thoughtful, driven professionals who want more than surface-level therapy. We help clients untangle patterns, build self-awareness, and create real, lasting change.



As the practice grows, we’re looking for someone who can help us run a tighter, smarter operation behind the scenes, while also ensuring that every client interaction feels smooth, thoughtful, and high-caliber.

This role is meant to be the engine of the business and to help it run smoother and more efficiently.



Position Overview

You’ll work closely with the founder to support both the day-to-day operations and the long-term growth of the practice.



Part of your role is execution: keeping things organized, handled, and moving forward. The other part is thinking: looking at how things are currently done and asking, “Is there a better way to do this?”



If you’re someone who enjoys building systems, improving workflows, and making things run more efficiently, this role will feel very natural to you.



This is a part-time role with the opportunity to grow in scope and responsibility as the practice expands



Core Responsibilities

1. Client Experience & Communication



You’ll help create a seamless, professional experience for every client interaction.



- Manage the shared inbox, including new inquiries and ongoing client communication

- Respond to inquiries in a timely, thoughtful, and organized way

- Coordinate scheduling changes, consultation requests, and general client needs

- Handle billing-related communication (e.g., declined cards, payment follow-ups)

- Support client retention by helping create consistent, high-quality touchpoints





2. Operations & Systems



You’ll help build the internal structure that allows the practice to grow without becoming chaotic.



- Create and refine systems, processes, and SOPs across the business

- Build and maintain internal documentation so tasks are clear and repeatable

- Ensure therapist notes and documentation are completed and up to date

- Update and maintain internal resources and workflows

- Identify inefficiencies or gaps in current systems and propose better solutions

- Support basic technical and platform coordination when needed





3. Growth & Visibility Support



You’ll support the systems that help the practice grow, without being responsible for full-scale marketing strategy.



- Maintain and update public profiles and directories (Google Business Profile, Yelp, etc.)

- Coordinate with the SEO specialist on content and visibility efforts

- Assist with content development (blog posts, worksheets, lead magnets, and other materials)

- Track and organize key metrics, including inquiries, consultations, and new clients

- Build and maintain a simple dashboard to monitor practice performance

- Conduct light competitor research and identify relevant opportunities (e.g., “best of” lists)

- Assist with website audits to ensure accuracy, clarity, and functionality





Who This Role Is For



This role is a strong fit for someone who:



- Is highly organized and naturally takes ownership of their work

- Enjoys both executing tasks and improving how those tasks are done

- Thinks critically and asks thoughtful questions instead of just following instructions

- Is reliable, detail-oriented, and consistent

- Communicates clearly and professionally in writing

- Is comfortable juggling multiple responsibilities and prioritizing effectively

- Likes being the person behind the scenes who makes everything run better



Confidentiality & Professional Responsibility



This role involves access to sensitive client information and protected health data. Maintaining strict confidentiality is essential.



- Handle all client information with care, discretion, and professionalism

- Follow established privacy and security practices at all times

- Exercise sound judgment when communicating internally and externally

title: TikTok Shop Growth Manager
description:
The Mission



We aren’t just another TikTok shop. We have  the best  physical tik tok studio  in the UK,  we have built a state-of-the-art, dedicated production studio—the best in the country—specifically to dominate TikTok Live and social commerce.

We have the stock, the infrastructure, and the world-class content. Now, we need the "Engine Room." We are looking for a high-level Growth Manager in the Philippines to take our digital empire to the next level. If you are a data-driven expert who thrives on scaling brands and hitting massive ROI targets, we want to talk to you.

What You’ll Be Doing (The Expectations)

• Ads Manager Command: You will own our TikTok Ads account. Your primary KPI is maintaining a 3.0+ ROI (ROAS) while scaling our daily spend. You must understand hook testing, audience layering, and Spark Ads.

• Affiliate Army Builder: You will lead a high-volume outreach strategy. Your goal is to get our products into the hands of hundreds of UK creators every month. You will manage the samples, the relationships, and the commissions.

• Content Architect: We provide the "raw" high-quality footage from our pro studio. You turn it into high-converting ads. You’ll sync trending audio, craft captions, and ensure every video is optimised for the "Yellow Basket."

• Shop Health Guard: You will be responsible for our Seller Center "Health Score." This means 24/7 monitoring, rapid customer responses, and ensuring 100% compliance with TikTok UK policies.

What We’re Looking For

• Proven UK Experience: You have managed UK-based TikTok Shops before and understand British shipping, VAT, and consumer vibes.

• Data Obsession: You don't just "post videos." You live in the analytics. You know why a 2.0 ROI dropped and exactly how to fix it.

• The Hustle: You are proactive. You don't wait for instructions; you see an opportunity to trend or a creator to message, and you take it.

• Fluent English: Your written communication must be perfect for our UK customer base.

The Rewards

• Competitive Base Salary: (Paid monthly in USD/PHP).

• The "Empire" Bonus: A performance-based commission on total shop revenue. When the brand hits the #1 spot, your bank account grows with us.

• Elite Resources: You’ll be working with the best studio content in the industry. No more trying to edit "blurry" phone clips.

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
WANTED: The World’s Most Relentless Sales Development Representative

Global | Remote | Healthcare & Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)



If you don’t love the grind, the scoreboard, and the win, do not apply.

We help independent healthcare practices (cardiology, nephrology, primary care, etc.) add predictable recurring revenue through remote patient monitoring — without adding more work to their already burnt-out staff.

Doctors are overwhelmed. Staff are drowning. Reimbursements are shrinking.

Our solution gives them lifesaving revenue and better care for their sickest patients. Your mission as our Sales Development Representative (SDR):

Turn cold contacts into hot, qualified, ready-to-buy opportunities — consistently, predictably, and at volume.

If you’re the kind of person who gets a dopamine hit from booking a qualified meeting, loves hearing “yes” more than you hate hearing “no,” and wants to be the engine of pipeline growth, keep reading.

If you just want a chill hourly gig and hate metrics... this will feel like punishment. Close this tab.



Why This Role Actually Matters

We are demand constrained, not delivery constrained.

When we get in front of the right practices, we win — and we keep them. But someone has to:

• Cut through the noise.

• Get past gatekeepers.

• Start real conversations with senior decision-makers at healthcare practices.

• Set high-intent, show-up-ready appointments for our Sales team.

That “someone” is you.

You’re not a “junior telemarketer.”

You’re a front-line revenue creator in a highly regulated, high-impact, high-ticket environment.



What You’ll Do (High-Impact Responsibilities) This is what your scoreboard will look like:

1. Own Outreach Like an Athlete Owns Training

• Hit and exceed daily activity targets:

o Minimum 50+ cold calls per day.

o Minimum 35+ targeted
s per day.

2. Turn Cold Prospects Into ?ualified Opportunities

• Speak to practice administrators, physician owners, and clinic leaders about

their staffing overload and revenue pain.

3. Handle Objections Like a Pro

• You will be trained — and expected — to turn these into conversations, not dead

ends.

4. Feed the Revenue Machine with Data G Feedback

• Log everything cleanly in HubSpot and Track: calls, connects, show rates,

conversion rates, and reasons for “no.”

5. Hit ?uotas — Then Break Them

• Consistently hit monthly meeting/qualified opportunity quotas.

You will not be “just setting meetings.”

You will be creating pipeline that turns into accounts and real-world clinical impact. 



What Sucks About This Job (On Purpose)

We’re going to be brutally honest:

• You will face rejection. A lot. Healthcare gatekeepers are pros. You’ll hear “not

interested,” “busy,” and voi
 more than you hear “yes.” If that crushes you,

this isn’t your game.

• High volume is non-negotiable. 50 calls and 35
s per day is the floor, not the

ceiling. “I’m more of a thinker than a doer” does not work here.

• You will be measured. Your activity, connects, meetings, show rates, and

opportunity quality will be tracked. If that stresses you out, we’ll stress each other

out.

• You must learn the healthcare world. This isn’t selling software to startups. You’ll

need to understand RPM, CPT codes at a basic level, and the pressure providers are

under.

• We move fast. Scripts, messaging, and targeting will evolve. If you cling to “the way

we’ve always done it,” you’ll be left behind.

If any of this makes you think, “That sounds intense, I just want something easy,” we are not for you.

If it makes you think, “Perfect, that’s where I outwork and outperform everyone else,” keep going.



What’s Amazing About This Job

Now the upside.

• You’re selling something genuinely valuable. This isn’t a gimmick. When we win a

practice, they get real incremental revenue and their sickest patients get better monitoring.

• Global-first, remote-first. You can work from anywhere with strong overlap to U.S. Eastern time. We care about output and attitude, not where your chair is.

• Above-market pay for true A-players.

o We benchmark compensation and aim to pay at least 10?ove the high-

water mark for top SDRs in your region.

o In emerging markets, that level of comp is life-changing — and we treat it

that way.

• Real coaching G growth. You’ll work closely with a team that knows the numbers and the craft. If you want to grow into senior SDR, AE, or sales leadership, this is a real launchpad.

• Mission + money. You get to earn great income while working on something that improves patient lives and helps struggling practices survive.



Who You Are (Non-Negotiables)

• Experience selling into healthcare is a MUST. You’ve sold to practices, hospitals,

or healthcare orgs before. You understand that world is different.

• Elite communicator — clear, confident, and human on the phone and in writing.

• Active listener. You don’t bulldoze; you hear what they’re actually saying and redirect wisely.

• Sales athlete. You love the game — the dials, the script tweaks, the scoreboard, the improvement.

• Resilient. High volume + rejection doesn’t drain you; it sharpens you.

• Organized and on top of your follow-ups, call notes, and tasks — not digging

through chaos every morning.

• Comfortable with sales tools and CRM (HubSpot) — or confident you can get up to speed quickly.

• A genuine team player: competitive, but not toxic; collaborative, not needy.

If you’ve never sold into healthcare and have no interest in learning that world, this role is

not for you.



How to Apply

We filter hard at the top of the funnel. If you can’t follow instructions here, you won’t make it in the role.

1.
 Subject Line (Exact Wording) Your subject line must be:

I actually read the instructions.

2. Attach Your Resume (Correct Format Only)

• Attach your resume as a PDF.

• File name must be:

[lastname].[firstname].sdr.cv.pdf

Example: doe.jane.sdr.cv.pdf

3. Animal ?uestion (In the
 Body)

Answer this in 3–5 sentences:

If you were an animal, what animal would you be and why — specifically as a Sales Development Representative?

We’re looking at how you think and communicate, not a “right” answer.

4. English Proficiency Link

Include a link to an English proficiency test result (Duolingo, IELTS, TOEFL, or similar).

5. Role-Specific Mini Task (Non-Negotiable)

In the same
, include:

• One sample cold
 (150 words max) you would send to:

A practice administrator at a cardiology clinic who is overwhelmed with staff workload and worried about declining reimbursements.

• One 3–4 bullet call opener you would use on a cold call to the same person. Plain text in the
 is fine. No design necessary. We care about clarity, relevance, and punch.

6. Send a 2-minute video on a time when you showed Perseverance in your life and/or career

7. Where to Send It

Send everything above to:



We only review candidates who do BOTH.

Anyone who misses any step is automatically filtered out.



What Happens Next (Including the Paid Trial)

1. We’ll review submissions that follow the instructions exactly.

2. If you look like a fit, we’ll invite you to a short interview focused on real conversations and scenarios, not generic “tell me about yourself” fluff.

3. Top candidates will be invited to a paid trial project, which may include:

o Running a small outbound sprint to a defined target list, o Using a script and messaging we co-create,

o Reporting your numbers and learnings.

This paid trial is the real evaluation, not your resume.

If you crush it, we’ll talk offer, ramp plan, and how you’re going to help us fill the pipeline

with high-value healthcare accounts.

Final CTA



If you’re:

• The kind of SDR who loves the grind, the game, and the win,

• Hungry to earn above-market pay for above-market performance,

• Excited to sell something that actually improves lives in the real world...

Then follow the instructions to the letter, send your application, and show us you’re the World’s Most Relentless Sales Development Representative.

title: Patient Recruiter
description:
WANTED: The World’s Most Reassuring Entry-Level Patient Recruiter

Global | Remote | Healthcare & Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)



If you can’t sound calm, confident, and kind on the phone, this is not your job.

We help independent medical practices (cardiology, nephrology, primary care, pulmonology, etc.) enroll their patients into Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) programs. These programs help patients with serious conditions stay safer at home — and help their doctors get paid for the work they’re already doing.

Your mission as our Entry-Level Patient Recruiter:

Call patients on behalf of their doctor, explain our program clearly, answer their questions, and confidently guide them to enroll in a service that genuinely helps them.

If you love talking to people, have a naturally reassuring voice, and can follow a script while still sounding human, you might be exactly who we’re looking for.

If you hate being on the phone, get flustered easily, or can’t stand repetitive calls... this will drain you. Don’t apply.



Why This Role Matters

Doctors trust us with their patients. Patients trust their doctors. You are the bridge between them.

When you do your job well:

• Patients understand why their doctor recommended this service.

• They feel safe, heard, and informed.

• More patients enroll, stick with the program, and get better health outcomes.

• Practices get the revenue they need to keep providing quality care.

You’re not a “telemarketer.”

You’re the first human voice patients hear from our company — and you set the tone for everything that comes after.



What You’ll Actually Do (Your Core Responsibilities)

You’ll be trained thoroughly and given scripts, FA?s, and support — but you’re responsible for making each call feel like a real conversation.

1. Direct Outreach to Patients

• Make outbound calls to patients whose doctors have identified them as good candidates for our remote monitoring program.

• Introduce yourself clearly, reference their practice/doctor, and set a calm, professional, friendly tone right away.

2. Follow the Script — Without Sounding Scripted

• Use our proven call scripts and talking points to explain:

o What the service is

o Why their doctor recommended it

o What they can expect (devices, calls, monitoring, etc.)

• Adapt your pacing and tone so you sound confident, relaxed, and natural, not robotic.

3. Answer ?uestions with Clarity and Patience

• Listen carefully to patients’ questions and concerns.

• Answer in simple, clear language — no jargon, no rushed explanations.

• Handle common questions about: privacy, costs, devices, time commitment, and

“Is this legit?”

4. Guide Patients Through the Enrollment Process

• Get verbal consent and complete the necessary steps to enroll them in the

program, following compliance guidelines.

• Confirm contact details, address, and any other required info accurately.

5. Document Your Work

• Log call outcomes, questions, and patient responses in our tools accurately and promptly.

• Flag any unusual concerns or feedback so our team can improve scripts and processes.

6. Show Up, Learn, Improve

• Join daily huddles or check-ins as scheduled.

• Participate in training and coaching to improve your calls.

• Take feedback as a tool, not an attack.



What Sucks About This Job (On Purpose)

We’re not sugarcoating this:

• Lots of calls. You will be calling patients back-to-back. If that drains you instead of

energizing you, you’ll burn out quickly.

• Repetition. You will say the same explanation many times a day. The skill is to

make it sound fresh every time.

• Some patients will be skeptical or irritated. They may be confused, worried about

scams, or tired of healthcare calls. You need emotional maturity to handle this

without taking it personally.

• You have to stick to the script and compliance rules. This isn’t improv. You must

follow certain phrases and guardrails to protect the patient and the company.

• You will be measured. Enrollments, call completion, and quality will all be tracked.

If metrics make you uncomfortable, this won’t be fun.

If you’re looking for a low-accountability, “do a little here and there” remote job... this is not it.

If you’re thinking, “I can handle all that and still bring good energy to every call,” keep reading.



What’s Amazing About This Job

Here’s why this can be an incredible opportunity for the right person:

• Real human impact. You’re helping patients access better monitoring and support

— often for serious chronic conditions.

• Entry-level but meaningful. You don’t need years of experience, but you’ll be doing important, visible work that affects patient care and practice revenue.

• Global-first, remote-first. Work from anywhere with stable internet and some overlap with U.S. Eastern time for calls and meetings.

• Above-market pay for A-players.

o We benchmark entry-level patient-facing roles by region and aim to pay at

least 10?ove the high-water mark in your area when performance is

strong.

o In emerging markets, that level of pay is truly life-changing compared to

typical call-center or BPO roles — and we treat it seriously. • Coaching G growth. You’ll learn:

o How to communicate with patients in a healthcare context o How to build trust quickly over the phone

o How the remote patient monitoring world works

• Long-term, you can grow into senior recruiter, patient success, customer support, or operations roles, depending on your strengths.



Who You Are (The Non-Negotiables)

You don’t need a medical degree or years in healthcare. You do need:

• A clear, warm, confident speaking voice in English.

• Comfort being on the phone for extended periods.

• Active listening skills — you hear what people are really asking, not just what they

say.

• Ability to stay calm and kind even when someone is confused, anxious, or

skeptical.

• Reliable internet, a quiet place to work, and a decent headset/mic.

• Willingness to learn the basics of our service and healthcare context — and

explain them simply.

Any prior experience in call centers, customer service, patient outreach, or telehealth is a plus, but not required if you’re a quick learner with the right attitude.



How to Apply

We use specific application steps to filter for people who can follow instructions — which is critical in a role like this.

If you skip any step below, your application will not be reviewed.

1.
 Subject Line (Exact Wording)

Your
 subject line must be:

I actually read the instructions. Anything else = automatic rejection.

2. Attach Your Resume (Specific File Name)

• Attach your resume as a PDF.

• File name format must be:

[lastname].[firstname].patientrecruiter.cv.pdf

Example: doe.jane.patientrecruiter.cv.pdf

3. Animal ?uestion (In the
 Body)

In the body of your
, answer this in 3–5 sentences:

If you were an animal, what animal would you be and why — specifically as a Patient Recruiter talking to patients over the phone?

We’re looking at how you think and express yourself.

4. English Proficiency Link

Include a link to an English proficiency test result (Duolingo, IELTS, TOEFL, or similar). If you don’t have one yet, choose one and complete it before applying.

5. Role-Specific Mini Task (Non-Negotiable)

Under the heading “Mini Task – Patient Recruiter” in your
 body, include:

1. CallOpenerScript(45–60seconds):

Write what you would say out loud at the start of a call to a patient whose doctor has recommended them for our remote monitoring service.

o Assume they don’t know who we are.

o Your goal: sound confident, relaxed, and trustworthy while explaining why

you’re calling.

2. Common ?uestion Response (3–5 sentences):

Answer this patient question in a way that is simple and reassuring:

“Is this going to cost me money?” Keep it realistic, clear, and friendly.

3. Send a 2-minute video on a time when you showed perseverance in your life and/or career.



7. Where to Send Your Application

Send your
 and attachments to: 



8. Paid Trial Project

For candidates who pass the first stages, we’ll invite you to a paid trial project, which may include:

• Practicing calls using a script

• Role-playing a series of patient conversations

• Demonstrating how you handle questions and stay calm and clear

This paid trial is the real evaluation, not your resume.



Final Call to Action

If you:

• Enjoy talking to people and helping them feel safe and informed

• Want an entry-level, remote role with real impact and above-market pay

• Can bring a calm, confident voice to every call, even on tough days

...then follow the instructions exactly, send your application, and show us you’re the World’s Most Reassuring Entry-Level Patient Recruiter.

title: HIRING: VIDEO EDITOR (FULL-TIME)
description:
Hey — I’m Karos. I’m 17, and I’m running a 7-figure e-commerce brand. This is not a corporate, LinkedIn-style job post. No fluff. No buzzwords. No BS. I’m looking for a full-time Video Editor who understands direct response and actually cares about performance. This is not a “just cut clips together” role. This is about making ads that perform.



I’m building fast and I’m obsessed with results. This is only the beginning. If you join now, you’re not just editing videos — you’re growing with me, long-term. This is NOT a short-term gig. It’s a chance to build something real together.



What you’ll be doing:

Editing direct-response video ads (Meta-focused)

Creating high-converting ads, not just aesthetic edits

Producing 3 hooks per video / per concept

Turning our raw ideas + strategy into scroll-stopping creatives

Using AI tools like ElevenLabs and AI Personas where it makes sense

Iterating based on performance data

Working directly with me — fast feedback, fast decisions

Helping push the brand from “doing well” to dominating



Highly valued skills:

- Graphic design experience. A strong eye for statics, headlines, and layouts. Experience making static ads that convert

- AI UGC: Experience with tools like HeyGen, Google Veo, Nano Banana

Candidates with either of these skill sets will have a strong advantage.



Who I’m looking for:

You’ve edited direct-response ads for e-commerce brands

You understand performance, not just visuals

You have a STRONG EYE for details

You’re fast, disciplined, and competitive

You care about results and high standards

You want to work exclusively on one brand and help grow it

If you want something slow, safe, and corporate — this isn’t for you.



Compensation:

Full-time, remote role

Base salary depends on experience and skill

Performance-based bonuses tied directly to ad results

For strong performers, monthly bonuses can realistically reach around $1,000, depending on output and performance. There’s no fixed cap — results drive upside. No guarantees, no rushing. If you make winning ads, you make more money. When the brand wins, you win.



The real opportunity:

This starts as a Video Editor role, but there’s room to grow. If you consistently deliver, understand what’s working, and show good judgment over time, you can move into a better role with more responsibility and higher pay. I’m building brands for the long run and looking for people who want stability, ownership, and multi-year collaboration, not constant job hopping.



Application process (important):

To filter unserious applicants, start your message with:

 “I create high-converting creatives.”

Then include:

Your experience with video editing & direct response ads

Whether you have static ad / graphic design experience

Links to relevant e-commerce ad work (portfolio)

Your availability and start date

Why you want to work with us



If this resonates and you’d thrive in this environment, D
 and I’ll get back to you. 

Speak soon, Karos
