title: Full Stack Developer
description:
About the Company



Ajaia is a rapidly growing AI consultancy and product studio dedicated to helping organizations move from experimentation to real results. We operate across four core pillars: AI Strategy and Advisory, AI Engineering and Automation, Workforce Training and Enablement, and Custom Product Development.



In addition to client services, Ajaia builds proprietary AI products, copilots, and vertical solutions that solve high-value industry problems. This dual model allows us to translate hands-on implementation experience into scalable products while continuously refining our approach through real-world deployment.



Our operating model combines deep technical execution with practical change management, enabling enterprises to deploy secure, compliant, and high-impact solutions that drive measurable operational value. From executive roadmapping to production-ready systems and workforce upskilling, Ajaia embeds intelligent tools directly into the workflows that matter most.



Mission: Help organizations win the AI moment.







Position Overview



As an AI-Native Full Stack Developer at Ajaia, you will build software across both client engagements and internal product initiatives where technical speed, adaptability, and execution quality matter.



You will be expected to take vague requirements, clarify the real problem, and ship robust solutions across web interfaces, backend services, integrations, and AI-driven workflows.



This is an individual contributor role suited for someone who can build with autonomy while collaborating closely with product, delivery, and client-facing teams. The role requires comfort working in fast-moving environments where priorities evolve and rapid iteration is expected.



Every role at Ajaia is AI-forward. We expect developers to use AI tools in daily workflows across research, implementation, debugging, testing, and documentation. AI usage is a core execution capability, not optional tooling.







Primary Responsibilities



Build and maintain full stack features for AI-enabled applications, internal platforms, and client delivery systems.

Work across frontend implementation, backend logic, API design, database operations, and cloud deployment.

Support the development of production systems that combine traditional software engineering with AI-powered capabilities.

Use AI tools regularly for coding support, debugging, technical research, test generation, and documentation improvement.

Collaborate with delivery leads, designers, and other engineers to translate priorities into production-ready software.

Navigate ambiguous project conditions, identify risks early, and unblock execution through pragmatic decision-making.

Contribute to code quality, reusable engineering patterns, and development process improvements.

Help ensure systems are maintainable, secure, observable, and ready for continuous iteration.



Job Requirements



Strong hands-on experience building and shipping full stack applications in professional environments.

Ability to implement responsive frontend interfaces and reliable backend services with strong attention to performance and quality.

Experience working with APIs, databases, authentication systems, cloud services, and modern deployment workflows.

Strong ownership mindset with the ability to operate effectively without highly detailed requirements.

Comfort managing multiple priorities or workstreams in a fast-moving consultancy or product environment.

Proficiency with GitHub and standard modern developer tooling used for collaboration and delivery.

Strong written and verbal communication skills when working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Active daily use of AI tools for planning, implementation, debugging, testing, and technical documentation.

Ability to critically evaluate AI-generated output and maintain a high standard for correctness and reliability.



Preferred Qualifications



Experience building AI-enabled product features or integrating large language models into applications.

Familiarity with workflow automation, prompt design, or applied LLM systems.

Experience working in startup, consultancy, or fast-iteration environments.



Schedule and Location



This is a fully remote role within approved hiring locations, subject to Ajaia hiring and compliance requirements.

Working schedule follows Eastern Time hours:

Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM ET, including holidays.



Technical Requirements



Reliable wired internet connection with at least 100 Mbps speed.

Computer or laptop with Intel Core i5 or Ryzen 5 processor or higher.

Minimum 8 GB RAM and at least 128 GB available storage.

Dual-monitor setup preferred.

Windows 10 or macOS (latest version).

1080p webcam and working microphone.

Backup solutions for power and internet interruptions.



Compensation and Benefits



Compensation: Competitive Base Salary + Performance Bonus

Remote work with earned flexibility.

Professional development opportunities.

Paid time off.



Why Join Ajaia



This role provides exposure to a wide range of applied AI and software delivery challenges while keeping you close to real implementation work.



You will have the opportunity to build across both client and internal initiatives, collaborate directly with operators and technical leaders, and develop the range that strong AI-native engineers need to stand out.



The environment rewards ownership, speed, and clarity rather than bureaucracy.



At Ajaia, you will work at the forefront of applied artificial intelligence and contribute to systems that reach real production environments. We expect all team members to be AI-native practitioners who actively use AI in their daily workflows to improve speed, quality, and outcomes.



Experience the Ajaia difference. Let’s build what’s next.

title: Credit Repair Specialist
description:
We are a growing startup on a mission to change lives through financial empowerment, and we are looking for a Credit Repair Specialist who wants to be more than just an employee—we want you to be a cornerstone of our family. Because we are in the early stages of our journey, we are intentionally building a culture rooted in mutual respect, open communication, and a genuine "team-first" dynamic.



You’ll be stepping into a high-impact role where your deep expertise in consumer law and your fluency in English will be the backbone of our client success. Whether you are managing the CRM or conducting sensitive client interviews, you won’t be just a cog in a machine; you’ll be a foundational partner helping us shape the soul of this company. If you’re looking for a workplace that feels like a tight-knit community rather than a corporate office, we’d love to have you help us write our first chapter.



Skills & Requirements

Expertise in Consumer Law: You possess a deep, working knowledge of the FCRA, FDCPA, and CROA. You don't just follow a script; you understand the legal leverage points that get results for clients.



Communication Mastery: You are fully fluent in English with a professional and empathetic phone presence. You are comfortable conducting client interviews and translating complex credit jargon into understandable advice.



Technical Proficiency: You have prior experience navigating Credit Repair CRMs and are comfortable maintaining organized, accurate digital records.



Adaptability & Growth Mindset: Since we are a new startup, your role will be dynamic. In the beginning, there may be "light" periods of credit repair work; during these times, you’ll be compensated for assisting with various administrative or operational tasks to help relieve the founder.



Flexible Availability: We are looking for someone comfortable starting part-time. As our client base grows, we intend for this role to evolve into a full-time position. While this transition is our ideal path, we are open to discussing what works best for your schedule as we scale.



The "Founding" Spirit: You are excited about the prospect of being the first of many. As the workload exceeds what one person can handle, you will play a role in helping us integrate and train new staff members.

title: Social media manager, reply-guy
description:
Hi! We're looking for a reliable, crypto-curious person to handle daily
/X engagement for our small DeFi project on the Stellar blockchain.

About Us

WhaleHub 
 on
/X) is a yield optimization protocol that helps crypto users earn better rewards on the Stellar network. We're early-stage, growing, and need someone consistent to represent us daily on Crypto
.

This is a simple, long-term role. If you show up every day and do good work, the role will grow — more responsibilities, more pay.

What You'll Do (30–60 minutes/day, 7 days/week)

Every day you'll receive a short task list via
. It takes 30–60 minutes to complete:



Post 5–7 reply comments on specific
/X accounts (comments are provided to you — you find the right post and paste them, adjusting slightly so they sound natural)

Like 10–15 posts from a list of ecosystem accounts

Retweet 2–3 positive ecosystem posts

If anyone replies to our comments or posts — respond with something short and friendly

Flag any DMs or important mentions to the founder

Send a quick screenshot of completed work



That's it. No content creation. No graphics. No strategy. Just consistent, daily execution.

What We Need From You

Required:



You understand basic crypto terms (tokens, staking, wallets, DeFi, blockchain)

You use
/X and understand how replies, likes, and retweets work

You are RELIABLE — this must happen every single day without reminders

Decent English — casual and natural, not formal

Available on
 for daily task delivery

Stable internet and a computer or phone



Bonus (not required):



You hold or have held crypto yourself

You know Stellar (XLM), AQUA, or any Stellar project

You follow Crypto
 and understand the culture

You've done social media work for a crypto project before



How It Works



Every day you get a
 message with the exact comments to post and accounts to engage with

First week: founder reviews everything before you post

After that: you run independently, founder spot-checks weekly

Weekly 10-minute check-in to make sure everything's on track



Pay



$150–200/month depending on experience



This role has a potential to grow.



How To Apply

Your experience with crypto — even personal counts (do you hold tokens? do you follow crypto
?)

Answer this: "What is a liquidity pool?" (1–2 sentences, your own words — don't copy from Google)

Link to your
/X profile (personal is fine)

When you can start



Short applications are fine. We care about reliability and crypto knowledge, not long cover letters.

This is a great entry point if you want to get into the crypto/web3 space. You'll learn DeFi, build connections in the Stellar ecosystem, and grow with the project.

Looking forward to your application

What We’re Looking For

title: Property Social Video Lead (TikTok/Reels) - Real Estate
description:
Remote | Contract (4–6 weeks) | Strong potential to grow into something bigger



Overview



We are a fast-growing real estate media company with a national photographer fleet, a growing client base, and a product that should be the most exciting thing happening in property media right now. 



We’re looking for someone who can build a repeatable short-form video format for property content that performs on TikTok and Instagram Reels, then turn it into a clear capture + edit playbook our photographers/editors can execute at scale.



What you’ll do



- Identify 2-3 best-in-class property/ lifestyle short-form formats and break down the performance mechanics (hook, pacing, retention, text strategy).

- Create or direct 1–2 benchmark videos to set the standard.

- Build a step-by-step playbook:

    - Required shot list + how to capture on location

    - Editing structure + pacing + text/audio rules

    - Do’s/don’ts + examples

- Make a short training (Loom/video + written guide) so the team can execute consistently.

- Recommend the next 2–3 formats to build and why they should perform.



You’re a strong fit if you…



- Have 3–5+ years creating organic short-form content that performs (TikTok/Reels/Shorts).

- Can explain why content works with specifics (not “it’s engaging”).

- Can shoot/direct capture and edit, or can brief editors extremely clearly.

- Think in systems: you can turn creative into a repeatable workflow/SOP.

- Move fast, test ideas, and iterate based on results.



Nice to have



- Real estate/interiors/architecture content experience.

- Experience building formats that other creators can replicate.



To apply (no CV needed)



1) Share 2 links to your best TikTok/Reels work (property/lifestyle preferred). What was your role (script/shoot/edit/direct)?  



2) In 2–3 sentences, why did one of those videos perform? (mechanics, not vibes)  



3) Link 1 best-in-class property short-form video you didn’t make + explain why it’s elite  



4) Your rate (project/day) + availability (hours/week) + start date

title: UGC Manager
description:
We are looking for a highly organized, proactive, and creator-savvy UGC Manager to lead our partnerships with user-generated content creators. This role combines talent sourcing, relationship management, creative direction, and operational excellence. You will be responsible for scouting top-performing creators, ensuring content aligns with brand goals, and managing the full UGC pipeline from ideation to delivery.



Key Responsibilities

1. Creator Sourcing & Partnerships

- Scout, vet, and recruit high-quality EU-based and US-based UGC creators that match our brand’s voice, audience, and creative needs.

- Build and maintain a strong network of reliable, high-performing creators.

- Negotiate rates, usage rights, and deliverables while staying within budget.

- Foster healthy long-term relationships and ensure excellent creator experience.



2. Creative Collaboration & Script Development

- Work closely with Creative Strategists to develop effective scripts, briefs, and content angles.

- Communicate clear creative expectations, guidelines, and references to creators.

- Provide ongoing creative direction during filming to ensure brand alignment.



3. Content Management & Quality Assurance

- Oversee creator filming timelines and ensure content targets are met.

- Facilitate revisions, retakes, or additional deliverables when needed.

- Review, evaluate, and approve all content before passing it to the internal team.

- Monitor performance of creator content to identify top performers for retainer or scaling.



4. Operations & Coordination

- Organize tracking sheets for creators, deliverables, deadlines, and budgets.

- Collaborate with Finance/Operations for timely payment processing.

- Maintain clear documentation and reporting for all creator partnerships.



Qualifications

- 1–3 years experience in UGC management, influencer marketing, partnerships, or talent management (agency or brand-side).

- Strong understanding of TikTok, Meta platforms, and current UGC trends.

- Excellent communication and negotiation skills.

- Highly organized with strong project management abilities.

- Ability to give clear creative feedback and understand performance-focused creative.

- Experience working cross-functionally with creative, ads, and finance teams.

- Comfortable managing multiple creators and deadlines simultaneously.



Nice-to-Haves

- Experience in eCommerce, DTC, or performance marketing.

- Background in content creation or video editing.

- Existing network of vetted UGC creators.



Who You Are

- Proactive, resourceful, and results-driven.

- A great communicator who builds relationships easily.

- Obsessed with quality content and always on top of social trends.

- Thrives in a fast-paced, creative environment.



Work Schedule:

Full time, Monday to Friday

Semi-flexible working hours



Benefits:

Competitive Salary

A fun, inclusive work environment with team events and activities.

Permanent work-from-home opportunity

Anniversary, birthday, and performance bonuses.



Please provide the following information to your application:

Share a link to your resume, and work portfolio (make sure it’s accessible).



Our Application Process:

Application > Initial Interview > Test Task > Final Interview

title: Construction Estimator & Contracts Administrator | Australian Industrial Projects | Full-Time WFH
description:
Full-Time | Permanent | Remote | Brisbane AEST Hours



Salary: Up to $2,500 USD/month for the right candidate — based on experience and demonstrated capability.



Hours: Monday to Friday | 40 hours per week | No night shifts, ever Holidays: Australian Public Holidays observed Start Date: As soon as the right person is found







About Us

We are a privately owned Australian property development and construction company based in South-East Queensland. We specialise in industrial warehouse projects delivered under a design-and-construct model — we acquire the land, obtain development approvals, manage the full construction, and then sell or lease the completed asset.



Our projects range in value from $2.5M to $15M AUD and we typically run 3 to 5 active projects simultaneously. We are a lean, high-performing team with zero tolerance for errors in cost or contract management. Every project runs to a defined system, with clear documentation, structured reporting, and direct accountability.



We are growing — and we are building the permanent team that will take us to the next level.







The Role

This is a senior, full-ownership position. You will be the commercial backbone of our construction projects — responsible for the financial and contractual control of multiple live projects simultaneously. You will work directly with the Director and Project Manager and will be expected to operate with a high degree of independence.



This is not a data-entry or support role. You will own the numbers, own the contracts, and own the outcomes.



Estimating & Cost Planning

• Preparing preliminary and detailed cost plans and budgets for industrial warehouse builds

• Completing full Bills of Quantities (BOQs) for each trade package

• Developing fully costed estimations across all construction works

• Maintaining project cost-to-complete forecasts and financial reports

• Assisting with cash-flow forecasts and cost reconciliation in MYOB



Contracts Administration

• Issuing and evaluating subcontractor tenders via Nexvia

• Preparing, negotiating, and executing subcontractor agreements

• Assessing and processing subcontractor progress claims

• Identifying, valuing, and managing variations end-to-end

• Ensuring all documentation meets QBCC, NCC, and Australian Standards requirements



Document Control

• Controlling and issuing all project documentation in accordance with project protocols

• Maintaining structured file control across OneDrive and Nexvia

• Reviewing drawings, quantities, and trade scopes for accuracy and completeness







What We Are Looking For

Essential Technical Skills

• 5+ years' experience in Contracts Administration and/or Quantity Surveying in the construction industry

• Qualification in Quantity Surveying, Construction Management, or Civil/Structural Engineering

• Demonstrated experience preparing Bills of Quantities and detailed cost plans

• Strong working knowledge of subcontractor agreements, progress claims, and variation management

• Proficiency in MS Excel and Bluebeam Revu (or equivalent takeoff software)

• Familiarity with Nexvia, Procore, or a similar construction project management platform

• Working knowledge of Australian construction standards (QBCC, NCC) — or a clear, demonstrable ability to learn them quickly



Essential Personal Attributes

• Exceptional attention to detail — you find errors before they become problems

• Self-directed and accountable — you take full ownership of your deliverables without needing to be chased

• Clear, confident communicator in written and spoken English — comfortable on video calls with the Director and subcontractors

• Able to manage multiple projects and competing deadlines without dropping the ball

• Quiet, dedicated home workspace with reliable internet and a documented power-outage contingency plan



Advantageous (Nice to Have)

• Prior experience working with Australian clients or on Australian construction projects

• Familiarity with tilt-panel and structural steel construction methods

• Experience with MYOB or similar accounting platforms

• Exposure to design-and-construct or developer-builder project delivery







What You Will Get

• Up to $2,500 USD/month — significantly above the Philippine market rate; we pay for quality

• Work on real, high-value projects — industrial warehouse builds valued between $2.5M and $15M AUD; this is not residential or fitout work

• Full commercial ownership — you are not a support role; you own the numbers, the contracts, and the outcomes

• Structured onboarding — comprehensive SOPs, Loom training videos, templates, and direct daily access to the Director during your first 90 days

• Learn Australian construction — deep exposure to QBCC compliance, NCC standards, Australian subcontract law, and design-and-construct delivery

• No night shifts, ever — consistent Brisbane AEST hours, Monday to Friday

• Long-term career, not a contract — we are building a permanent team; as the business grows, senior roles will be created from within

• Travel to Australia — high performers will be invited to visit our projects on-site for genuine hands-on experience in Australian construction







How to Apply

We read every application carefully. To be considered, please follow these steps exactly — attention to detail starts here.



Step 1 — Submit your application via this platform 

           — And
 it to 
 



The subject line:  "BOQ Ready – [Your Full Name]"



In your cover message, include:

• A 3–4 sentence description of a time you improved accuracy or efficiency in a previous role

• Your documented power-outage contingency plan (internet and power backup)



Step 2 — Record a 2-minute video introduction covering:

• Your background in construction, cost management, and contracts administration

• One specific achievement you are proud of that demonstrates accuracy or problem-solving

• A description of your workspace setup



Step 3 — Attach the following:

• Resume (PDF format)

• Photo of your workspace

• SpeedTest.net screenshot (taken from your primary work location)

• Computer specifications (processor, RAM, internet type, backup devices)



Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.







What Happens Next

1 All applications reviewed within 5 business days of submission

2 Shortlisted candidates invited to complete a practical skills assessment (cost planning and contracts administration task)

3 Video interview with the Director

4 Reference checks and formal offer







We are a company that rewards accuracy, accountability, and initiative. If you are a senior construction commercial professional who wants a long-term career with a growing Australian developer-builder — we want to hear from you.

title: Experienced Associate Brand Manager to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring 2 Associate Brand Manager who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media

Our flagship product is a flavored air device — a clean, modern, and satisfying alternative to old routines — and we’re scaling fast across global markets.



We’re now building one of Europe’s most effective creative marketing teams and are searching for a Video Editor who can turn ideas into scroll-stopping, high-converting VSLs and Meta ad creatives.



If you love storytelling, psychology, and data — and you’re obsessed with crafting videos that sell — this might be your next big opportunity.





What You’ll Do

- Edit short-form VSLs (2–3 mins) and UGC-style ads for
, Instagram & TikTok

- Turn testimonials, product shots, and voiceovers into conversion-focused video assets

- Produce multiple ad variations for A/B testing and performance optimization

- Source high-quality UGC, B-roll, and sound effects from TikTok, YouTube, or stock sites

- Use AI tools (avatars, voiceovers, B-roll generation, etc.) to boost creative output

- Work closely with our creative strategist and performance team to test and iterate fast

- Keep up with trends on Reels, TikTok & YouTube Shorts in the wellness/lifestyle niche



What We’re Looking For

- 1–2+ years of proven experience editing VSLs or performance ads for DTC/eCom brands

- Solid understanding of direct-response marketing & buyer psychology

- Skilled in CapCut Pro, Premiere Pro, or similar tools

- Experience using or open to learning AI video tools (HeyGen, VEO 3, ElevenLabs, etc.)

- Ability to source and curate UGC or short-form content independently

- Strong communicator — reliable, organized, and proactive

- Excellent English (written & spoken)



This Role Isn’t for You If…

- You care only about aesthetics, not results and performance data

- You can’t handle tight deadlines or fast feedback loops

- You’ve never edited Meta VSL ads or don’t understand DTC ad structure

- You need constant supervision or hand-holding

- You’re not available 10–20 hours per week on a consistent basis



Why You’ll Love Working With Us

- Be part of a high-performance, remote-first team that values speed & creativity

- Direct access to experienced creative strategists and media buyers

- Real ownership — your edits directly drive revenue & growth

- Freedom to experiment with AI tools, trends & creative strategies

- Long-term growth opportunity with a fast-scaling European brand

- Competitive pay + performance-based bonuses for top results



Ready to Apply



- We only review applications submitted via Typeform.

title: Video Editor for Fast-Growing DTC Wellness Brand (VSL & AI-Focused)
description:
About Us

We are a fast-scaling direct-to-consumer health & wellness brand based in Europe, selling across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Scandinavia. Our growth is driven by paid social — primarily Meta ads — and we're doubling down on long-form video ads that sell.

We move fast. We test relentlessly. And we need someone who can keep up.



The Role

We're hiring an AI Video Ad Editor who specializes in creating high-converting Video Sales Letters (VSLs) using AI tools. You'll be our go-to person for producing persuasive, long-form video ads that educate, build trust, and drive sales — all created with AI-powered workflows.

If you've ever watched a VSL and thought "I could make this 10x faster with AI" — this role is for you.



What You'll Be Creating

VSLs (Video Sales Letters): This is your bread and butter. Long-form (2-10 min) persuasive video ads combining AI voiceovers, generated visuals, motion graphics, text overlays, and b-roll — engineered to convert

Advertorial-Style Video Ads: Mid-length educational ads that feel like content, not advertising

Hook Variations: Multiple opening sequences per VSL for aggressive split testing

UGC-Style Ads & Street Interviews: AI-generated testimonial and social proof style content to support VSL campaigns

Cartoon & Animated Ads: AI-generated animated ad variations when needed





What We're Looking For

Must-Have:

Proven experience creating VSLs or long-form video ads for direct response, e-commerce, or info-product brands

Deep, daily use of AI video tools (Runway, Kling, Pika, HeyGen, Synthesia, D-ID, or similar)

Experience with AI image generation for ad visuals (Midjourney, DALL-E, Flux, Leonardo, or similar)

You understand VSL structure — hook, problem, agitation, mechanism, proof, offer, CTA

You know how to pace a video to keep viewers watching — transitions, pattern interrupts, visual storytelling

Ability to deliver 10-15+ ad creatives per week

Fluent English communication

Reliable internet and own setup



What You Get

Stable, long-term position with a rapidly growing brand

title: Performance Video Specialist – Meta Ads & VSL (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
About Us

We are a fast-scaling direct-to-consumer health & wellness brand running Meta campaigns across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Scandinavia. We spend serious money on paid social — and we need creative that earns it back.

We don't separate marketing from creative.

The best video ads are made by people who understand both.



The Role

You think in ROAS. You've watched enough Meta ad accounts to know that creative is the primary lever — not targeting, not budget, not bidding strategy. You understand that a bad hook kills an ad in 2 seconds flat, and that the difference between a 1.5x and a 3x ROAS is often one sentence in the opening frame.

You can edit video — but more importantly, you can evaluate video. You know when an ad will work before it runs. You've seen enough winning and losing creatives to have pattern recognition, and you use that to build better assets every week.

You'll work directly with the founder, own our video creative output, and be a key voice in which concepts get produced and which get killed.



What You'll Be Producing



VSLs for Meta: Conversion-focused video sales letters optimized for cold traffic on
 and Instagram

Short-form creatives: Hook-first 30–90 second ads for Reels, Stories, and Feed

Creative iterations: Fast variations of winning ads — new hooks, new CTAs, new angles

UGC and testimonial videos: Social proof assets built from raw footage or AI-generated content

Performance reports: Basic weekly feedback on which creatives are pulling and why





What You Understand Without Being Told



Hook strength is the most important variable in any video ad

Cold traffic and warm traffic need completely different creative

The comment section tells you more about creative quality than any dashboard

Fatigue is inevitable — volume and variation are the answer

A 4% CTR with a 1.5 ROAS is worse than a 1.5% CTR with a 3.5 ROAS





Requirements



2+ years working with video creative for Meta 
 & Instagram) paid campaigns

Hands-on experience producing or editing VSLs or direct-response video ads

Strong understanding of Meta ad creative best practices and performance metrics

Experience using AI tools for video production (avatars, voiceovers, B-roll generation)

Proficient in CapCut Pro, Premiere Pro, or similar editing software

Able to produce and iterate on 8–12 creatives per week

Comfortable discussing creative performance with data — CTR, hook rate, ROAS, CPP

Fluent written English

Reliable internet and own equipment



Bonus:



Experience managing or closely supporting Meta ad accounts

Familiarity with creative testing frameworks (ABO, CBO, holdout tests)

Background in DTC health, wellness, or supplement brands





This Role Is NOT For You If:



You've only edited video without ever looking at whether it converted

You're uncomfortable being evaluated on performance metrics

You separate "the creative side" from "the marketing side" in your mind

You've never produced a VSL or direct-response video ad

You prefer brand content and storytelling over direct-response and conversion





What We Offer



Full-time remote position with direct access to the founder

Front-row seat to a scaling European D2C brand — see exactly how spend, creative, and ROAS interact

Performance bonuses on winning creatives — your best work is rewarded directly

Access to all paid tools and subscriptions

Real creative ownership and influence over which concepts get produced

Long-term growth opportunity as the brand scales across new markets and products





How to Apply

Only Typeform applications are reviewed. Copy and paste the link (remove spaces):

form .typeform. com/to/ dO9pZYvu

Under 8 minutes. We respond within 3–5 business days.

title: Direct Response Video Editor – Sales Copy & VSL (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
About UsWe are a fast-scaling direct-to-consumer health & wellness brand running paid media across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Scandinavia. Our growth engine is Meta — and the creative that drives it is the VSL.Most video editors understand cuts and transitions.



We need someone who understands why people buy.



The Role

You started with words — copy, scripts, sales pages — and somewhere along the way you discovered that video was just direct response with a play button. Now you edit with a copywriter's brain: every cut serves the argument, every B-roll clip reinforces the hook, every pacing decision moves the viewer closer to the CTA.That's exactly who we're looking for.You'll take our briefs and scripts and turn them into VSLs that convert cold traffic on Meta. You'll work directly with the founder, iterate fast based on performance data, and own creative output from first frame to final export.What You'll Be Producing



VSLs (4–20 min): Problem-agitate-solve structured video ads built around our direct-response scripts

Hook variations: Multiple cold opens testing different angles, emotions, and entry points

Short-form cutdowns: 30–90 second versions of winning long-form VSLs for Reels and Stories

UGC-style ads: Authentic-feeling social proof and testimonial videos



Overlay and subtitle packages: Animated captions, callouts, and emphasis overlays for Meta

What Makes You DifferentYou don't just receive a script — you improve it. You flag weak transitions, suggest stronger hooks, and notice when the argument loses 
ntum at minute three. You've read enough Ogilvy, Halbert, or Schwartz to know that great video ads are great sales letters first.You understand:



Why curiosity gaps keep viewers watching

How to structure a problem-agitate-solve arc visually

When to slow down and when to punch through

How B-roll either supports or undermines the copy beneath it



Requirements

2+ years editing direct-response video ads or VSLs for DTC or info product brands

Strong understanding of sales copy structure and direct-response principles

Proficient in Premiere Pro, CapCut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

Experience with AI voiceover tools: ElevenLabs, PlayHT, or similar

Able to source B-roll, overlays, and sound design independently

Produces 3–5 polished VSL variants per week

Fluent written English

Reliable internet and own equipment



Bonus:

You've written copy yourself — scripts,
s, landing pages

Experience with HeyGen, Synthesia, or AI avatar tools

Knowledge of Meta ad creative best practices

This Role Is NOT For You If:

You've only edited brand content, social content, or YouTube vlogs

You wait for detailed frame-by-frame direction before starting

You don't read the script before you open the timeline

You've never thought about why a video did or didn't convert



What We Offer

Full-time, stable long-term remote position

Direct access to the founder — no layers, no bottlenecks

Performance bonuses tied to winning creatives

Structured briefs and scripts — you focus on execution

Access to all paid tools and subscriptions



A brand actively scaling across Europe — your work runs at real budget



How to Apply

Only applications submitted via our Typeform are reviewed. Copy and paste the link (remove spaces):form .typeform. com/to/ dO9pZYvuUnder 8 minutes. We respond within 3–5 business days.

title: VSL Producer – Direct Response Video Specialist (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
VSL Producer – Direct Response Video Specialist (Full-Time, Remote)

Company: Fast-Growing European D2C Brand (Health & Wellness)



About Us

We are a fast-scaling direct-to-consumer health & wellness brand running paid media across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Scandinavia. Our primary acquisition channel is Meta — and our primary creative weapon is the VSL.

We don't need a video editor who "also does VSLs."

We need someone who thinks in VSLs.



The Role

You will own VSL production end-to-end. From script brief to final export — you turn our direct-response copy into high-converting video sales letters that run on Meta and scale.

You understand Eugene Schwartz's stages of market sophistication. You know why the first 3 seconds either make or break a $10,000/day ad spend. You've watched enough winning VSLs to recognize a strong hook pattern before the voiceover even finishes.

This is a performance role. Your output is measured in ROAS, not views.



What You'll Be Producing



Long-form VSLs (8–20 min): Authority-driven, problem-agitate-solve structured video ads with AI voiceovers, AI avatars, B-roll, and animated overlays

Short-form VSLs (2–4 min): Punchy, hook-first Meta feed and Reels variants

Hook libraries: 5–10 cold open variations per VSL for systematic A/B testing

VSL cutdowns: Reformatted 30/60s versions from winning long-form VSLs

Advertorial-style video: Scrollable "news article" format videos for pre-lander traffic





You Know Exactly What We Mean By:



Problem-agitate-solve structure

Open loop hooks and curiosity gaps

Pattern interrupt at the 30-second mark

Social proof stacking

Objection handling before the CTA

False close and re-open

Urgency stack at the end



If you had to Google any of those terms — this role isn't for you.



Technical Requirements



2+ years producing VSLs specifically for DTC health, wellness, or info product brands

Proficient in CapCut Pro, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

Hands-on with AI avatar tools: HeyGen, Synthesia, D-ID, or similar

AI voiceover experience: ElevenLabs, PlayHT, or similar

Able to source and cut B-roll, stock footage, and overlays independently

Comfortable receiving a script + brief and delivering a publish-ready VSL

Able to produce 3–5 complete VSL variants per week

Fluent in written English

Reliable internet and own equipment



Bonus if you have:



Experience with Pictory, Descript, or similar AI-assisted editing tools

Knowledge of subtitle/caption styling for Meta (burned-in, animated)

Experience editing for European health/wellness brands





This Role Is NOT For You If:



You've never produced a VSL longer than 5 minutes

You edit for aesthetics first and conversion second

You need a creative director to guide every decision

You've never tracked whether your video actually converted

You're looking for creative freedom without performance accountability





What We Offer



Full-time, stable long-term position

Work directly with the founder — zero bureaucracy

Performance bonuses tied to winning VSLs (we track ROAS per creative)

Access to all paid tools and subscriptions

Ongoing VSL briefs with structured scripts — you focus on production, not ideation

A brand that is actively scaling — your best work will run at serious budget





How to Apply

We only review applications submitted via our Typeform. Copy and paste the link below (remove spaces):

form .typeform. com/to/ dO9pZYvu

Only completed applications are reviewed.

Takes under 8 minutes — we respond within 3–5 business days.

title: Junior Media Buyer
description:
ABOUT SPARKBLAZE DIGITAL

Sparkblaze Digital is a forward-thinking digital solutions agency that helps brands ignite their online presence through creative marketing, strategic content, and efficient digital operations. We empower businesses to grow and scale through innovation, collaboration, and passion for excellence.



WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?

We are looking for a Junior Media Buyer to support the execution, monitoring, and optimization of paid advertising campaigns across digital platforms. This role is ideal for someone who understands the fundamentals of paid ads and is eager to grow in performance marketing.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

title: Amazon PPC Manager
description:
Job Title: Amazon PPC Manager (Remote, Philippines)



We are hiring an Amazon PPC Manager to take ownership of paid advertising across multiple Amazon brands.



This is not a task-following role.

We are looking for someone who thinks critically, understands profitability, and can explain why performance changes — not just report numbers.



If you are someone who:

• Notices patterns in data before others do

• Questions assumptions instead of blindly executing

• Understands that ACOS alone means nothing

• Enjoys solving problems and improving systems



…you may be a strong fit.



What You’ll Do



• Own Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, and Sponsored Display performance

• Identify wasted spend and reallocate budgets intelligently

• Expand keywords and targeting using structured logic

• Analyze performance trends and explain root causes

• Recommend strategic changes based on profitability, not vanity metrics

• Provide short weekly summaries of what changed, why, and what’s next



What We Care About



• Clear thinking and strong logic

• Ability to explain decisions simply

• Attention to detail across multiple accounts

• Reliability and responsiveness

• Ownership mindset (not waiting to be told what to do)



Basic Requirements



• 2+ years Amazon PPC experience

• Comfortable with spreadsheets and data analysis

• Strong written English

• Stable internet and ability to work full-time remotely



To Apply



Send:

A short intro about yourself

Your PPC experience and typical account size

The biggest PPC mistake you see brands make

Expected monthly salary in USD

Availability (part time, full time, etc)



Applicants who submit thoughtful REAL answers will be prioritized. AI slop will be auto deleted.

title: Amazon FBA Seller Central Marketing Analyst / Manager / Specialist
description:
Amazon FBA Seller Central Marketing Analyst / Manager / Specialist



+4 Years Amazon Experience Required



This is a unique opportunity for an organized and entrepreneurially-minded Amazon Manager to join our team and help operate and grow startup and household name client brands. As an Amazon Manager, you will be ideating and executing growth strategies while optimizing the Amazon presence of our rapidly growing portfolio of client products and brands.



This is a full time remote role.



What you’ll be doing:



Playing an integral role in designing and building a best-in-class Amazon presence for a set of companies from our diverse brand portfolio.



Creating and continually optimizing listings, as well as troubleshooting and fixing issues as they arise.



Developing marketing strategies across our portfolio of brands, and coordinating with stakeholders as needed to execute.



Managing promotions (coupons, deals) and PPC advertising campaigns.



Analyzing Amazon data (sales, conversation rate, sessions, etc.) to determine why certain products are trending up or down and developing strategies to increase conversions and sales.



Resolving issues that come up (listing take downs, content not publishing, catalog errors, etc.) and escalating to internal stakeholders as needed.



Developing and maintaining a professional knowledge of new or emerging trends within the Amazon and online shopping space. You’ll constantly research opportunities for improvement to drive traffic and conversion growth.





Attending weekly meetings to communicate needs and resolve issues.





Your profile / Must Have:



In-depth knowledge of Amazon Seller Central and FBA processes. +4 Years experience with a track record of success.

Comprehensive understanding of Amazon and Seller Central, best practices, along with policies and guidelines. Flat file expert.



Proven experience as an Amazon Virtual Assistant or in a similar role.

Experience in Amazon detail page optimization, listing management, SEO marketing, and PPC

Excellent organizational and analytical skills, as well as a hands-on mentality.

Excellent communications and customer service skills

Excellent English (speaking and writing)

Creative thinker



Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and make informed decisions. Top notch problem solving skills.



Attention to detail. Self-motivated and able to work independently.

Good with Excel, Google Sheets, and formulas

Proficient in using Google Drive for collaborative tasks.





Nice to Have



Experience with graphic design / photoshop or similar software



Experience with social media marketing



Experience with Walmart Seller Central





About us

We are a full-service agency that works with brands and manufactures to grow their sales and profit on Amazon both in the US and globally. Additionally, we own a handful of private label brands that we own and operate ourselves.

This is a remote position, and candidates from all locations are welcome to apply. We offer flexible working hours and opportunities for professional growth within our organization.



To apply, please submit your resume and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience and why you are interested in this position. We look forward to reviewing your application!

title: Amazon OA Sourcer and Admin
description:
Compensation: 60,000 PHP / Month, Performance Bonuses

Location: Fully remote

Schedule: Monday to Friday, Flexible Hours

Employment Type: Full-time

Pay Frequency: Bi-Weekly



Job Overview



We're hiring an experienced Online Arbitrage (OA) Sourcer and Admin to join a growing Amazon FBA business currently doing $1M+ in annual revenue. Sourcing is your primary job, 80% of your time will be spent finding, evaluating, and purchasing profitable leads. The remaining 20% will be handling admin work including inventory reconciliation, prep center communication, and Amazon account health monitoring.



You'll be the first full-time hire on this team, which means you have a real opportunity to grow into a lead role. Once you've proven yourself, you'll have the chance to help bring on and manage additional sourcers underneath you. If you're a seasoned OA sourcer who wants to be trusted, well-compensated, and given room to grow, this is your role.





Responsibilities?

Source profitable OA leads daily using tools like SellerAmp and Keepa

Evaluate leads using sales rank history, price trends, offer counts, and margin analysis before submitting

Submit qualified leads into a structured Google Sheet pipeline

Purchase leads and encode billing and shipping information accurately and consistently

Hit daily and monthly spend targets with minimal supervision (starting goal: $50K/month)

Stay in regular contact with the prep center to sort out any inventory or receiving issues on your own

Keep inventory records reconciled between the prep center and Amazon, and flag anything that looks off

Keep sourcing and purchasing data organized and up to date in the existing tracking systems

Monitor account health in Seller Central for IP complaints

Track FBA inbound shipments and bring any missing or short quantities to management's attention so invoices and appeals can be filed on time

Bring your own sourcing approach to the table, if something works better than what we're doing, we want to know

As the team grows, help bring new sourcers up to speed and take on a lead role



Requirements

2+ years of hands-on OA sourcing experience for Amazon FBA

Strong proficiency with Keepa, you know how to read a chart and make a confident buying decision

Experience using sourcing tools such as Tactical Arbitrage, Seller Amp, or equivalent

Able to find 3-5+ viable leads per hour and maintain consistent daily output

Trustworthy and professional when handling sensitive payment and financial information

Highly organized and detail-oriented with a strong ability to manage multiple tasks without dropping the ball



Solid knowledge of Amazon Seller Central operations including inventory management, account health, and shipment workflows

Experience with prep center communication



Experience with Amazon inventory reconciliation

Familiarity with the full OA purchasing process from lead sourcing to order placement

Reliable internet connection and a distraction-free work setup



Nice to Have (Not Required)

Experience managing or mentoring other sourcers

Familiarity with prep center workflows and FBA shipment reconciliation

Experience using AI-assisted scraping tools or automation for lead generation

Comfortable building or refining sourcing SOPs and processes

Experience with Google Sheets, including formula-based tracking systems



What to Expect in Your First 30 Days

Complete onboarding and review our existing systems, SOPs, and lead pipeline setup

Watch our internal Sourcing 101 training course, even experienced sourcers have found it valuable

Check-in call with our team to close any knowledge gaps and align on expectations

Begin sourcing immediately, the infrastructure is already built, you plug in and run

Admin work is minimal at first while you find your footing, focus is entirely on sourcing quality and consistency



To Apply

title: Commercial Solar Admin (Australia)
description:
We are seeking an Australian Commercial Solar Admin Specialist with hands-on experience in Australian commercial solar projects (30 kW and above).

This role is not suitable for general VAs or candidates without direct Australian solar experience.



Key Responsibilities:



1. Commercial Solar Administration

- Manage DNSP grid connection applications for commercial solar systems (30 kW+)

- Prepare and submit pre-approval applications

- Respond to DNSP technical queries and requests for additional information

- Manage export limits, zero-export requirements, and protection conditions

- Upload and manage documentation across DNSP portals



2. Solar + Battery Projects

- Support commercial battery approvals and documentation

- Ensure batteries are included correctly in DNSP approvals and SLDs

- Manage commissioning evidence and compliance documentation

- Coordinate with installers to resolve approval or documentation gaps



3. Compliance & Documentation

Review and manage:

- Single Line Diagrams (SLDs)

- CEC-compliant documentation

- Installer and designer accreditation evidence

- Commissioning photos and reports

Prepare STC documentation for commercial systems

Package compliance folders for audit readiness



Coordination & Communication

- Australian installers and electricians

- STC traders

- Retailers and DNSPs (via
 and portals)

Track job status, approvals, and outstanding actions

Flag risks, delays, or compliance issues early and escalate appropriately



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)



Candidates must have:

- Direct experience working with Australian solar companies

- Experience supporting commercial solar systems (30 kW+)

- Hands-on experience with at least one of the following:

- Grid connection applications

- STCs for commercial solar

- Australian DNSP portals



A solid understanding of:

- Export limits and DNSP pre-approval requirements

- Solar + battery integration fundamentals

- Clean Energy Council (CEC) compliance frameworks



Essential:

Victorian solar experience, including exposure to Victorian DNSPs such as:

- Powercor

- AusNet

- Jemena

- United Energy

- Experience across multiple Australian states (VIC, NSW, QLD)

- Experience managing commercial metering coordination

- Familiarity with CRMs such as Pylon, OpenSolar, ServiceM8, or similar

- Experience supporting mid-to-high volume solar installers



*Candidates without Australian solar experience should not apply.*



Benefits

Why You’ll Love Working With Us

- AU time zone working hours (no graveyard shifts)

- We’re a passionate, forward-thinking team (and genuinely good humans)

- Growth opportunities as we expand

- Supportive culture where initiatives and ideas are celebrated

title: Systems Operator
description:
# Systems Operator — IT Consultancy (Full-Time, Remote)



## About Us



We are a boutique Australian IT consultancy based in Sydney, Australia. We've been operating since 2000, providing CRM management, managed cloud services, and business intelligence to enterprise and government clients. Our clients are long-term (some since 2005) and we pride ourselves on reliability and quality.



We're looking for a Systems Operator to join our small team. This is not a developer role. This is a technical operations role — you'll be the person who keeps client IT systems running, follows documented procedures, handles support requests, and makes sure nothing falls through the cracks.



## What You'll Do



**Client Support (40%)**

- Triage incoming client support tickets and resolve routine requests

- Escalate complex or unusual issues with clear notes on what you've checked

- Communicate with Australian clients via
 — professional, clear English is essential

- Follow up on open tickets so nothing goes stale



**System Operations (30%)**

- Monitor Docker containers — check they're running, restart when needed, read logs for errors

- Manage CRM user accounts — create users, update permissions, reset passwords

- Publish and manage BI reports and dashboards (Metabase) following our security checklists

- Process data files — receive CSVs from clients, import into CRM, verify results

- Execute EDM 
 marketing) campaigns following existing templates and procedures



**Administration (20%)**

- Maintain our password manager (Bitwarden) — add credentials, manage groups

- Keep our Google Drive organised — file documents in the correct client folders

- Upload and manage recordings from client meetings

- Update our delivery tracking board with task status



**Process & Documentation (10%)**

- Follow our runbooks and checklists for every task (we have documented procedures for everything)

- Flag when a procedure is missing or doesn't cover a situation you encounter

- Log all changes to client systems using our change control templates



## What We're Looking For



**Must have:**

- Excellent written English — you'll be
ing Australian clients directly

- Experience with CRM systems (any CRM — SuiteCRM, SugarCRM, HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoho, etc.)

- Comfortable with Linux command line basics (SSH, navigating directories, reading logs, restarting services)

- Experience with Docker at a basic level (docker ps, docker restart, docker logs — not building images)

- Able to work with databases at a basic level (running provided SQL queries, checking results — not writing complex queries from scratch)

- Reliable, self-directed, and communicative — if you're stuck, you tell us immediately, you don't sit on it

- Detail-oriented — our clients trust us with sensitive data, accuracy matters every time

- Available to work Australian business hours: 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Sydney time (AEST/AEDT), Monday to Friday



**Nice to have:**

- Experience with Metabase or similar BI tools

- Familiarity with n8n, Zapier, or similar automation platforms

- Basic HTML/CSS (enough to adjust an
 template, not build a website)

- Experience in an IT support or managed services environment

- Experience with Git (pulling repos, reading markdown files)



**We do NOT need:**

- A software developer — this is not a coding role

- A designer — we handle creative separately

- Someone who wants to "move into development" — this role is operations and it stays operations

- A project manager — we manage the projects, you execute within them



## How We Work



- We use documented procedures for everything. If there's a runbook, you follow it. If there isn't one, you flag it and we write one together.

- We use Bitwarden for passwords, Google Drive for files, GitHub for documentation, and a Kanban board for task tracking.

- Communication is primarily async 
, messaging) with a weekly video call. But because you're working Sydney hours, real-time collaboration happens naturally throughout the day.

- We value reliability over heroics. Doing the right thing consistently beats occasional brilliance.

- We treat our team well and expect the same in return. Long-term engagement, not a revolving door.



## Details



- **Hours**: Full-time (40 hours/week), 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Sydney time (AEST/AEDT), Monday to Friday

- **Public holidays**: You follow the **Australian public holiday calendar**, not the Philippine one. When Australia has a public holiday, you have a day off. When the Philippines has a public holiday but Australia doesn't, it's a normal working day. This is non-negotiable — our clients expect coverage during Philippine holidays.

- **Compensation**: PHP 42,000 – 50,000/month (approximately AUD $1,000 – $1,200/month) depending on experience

- **Start**: Within 2-4 weeks of offer

- **Equipment**: You provide your own computer and reliable internet. We provide all accounts, tools, and access.

- **Trial period**: 2-week paid trial to make sure the fit works for both sides



## How to Apply



Send me a message with:



1. A short summary of your relevant experience (2-3 paragraphs, not a novel)

2. Describe a time you followed a documented process to resolve a client issue — what was the process, what happened, what did you do?

3. Describe a time you found a gap in a process or procedure — what was missing and what did you do about it?

4. Your expected monthly salary in PHP

5. Confirm you can work 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Sydney time, Monday to Friday, following Australian public holidays (not Philippine ones)



Please do not send a generic cover letter. I will read every response that answers these five questions. I will not read responses that don't.

title: WordPress Developer | Mid-Senior Level
description:
About the Role

We are hiring a WordPress Developer with experience in user-centered design, AI-assisted development, CRM integration, workflow automation, and WooCommerce.

We already have a working WordPress website, a live WooCommerce store, and some existing CRM integrations in place. This is not a build-from-scratch role. We need someone who can step in, understand what we have, and take it further — improving the user experience, expanding CRM and automation capabilities, and maintaining what is already running.



Responsibilities

Web Design and User Experience

Review and improve the existing WordPress website with a focus on usability, navigation, and conversion

Apply UX principles such as visual hierarchy, cognitive load management, and common scanning patterns to optimize current page layouts

Ensure responsive, mobile-first performance across devices and browsers

WooCommerce (Existing Store)

Maintain and optimize the existing product pages, checkout flows, and payment/shipping configurations

Troubleshoot store issues and improve performance

AI-Assisted Development

Use AI coding tools such as Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, or similar platforms to support development, debugging, and code generation

Integrate AI-driven features into the website where applicable 

Stay informed on new AI development tools and evaluate them for practical use

CRM Integration — Zoho CRM and GoHighLevel (Expand Existing Setup)

Review and improve existing CRM integrations between WordPress and Zoho CRM and/or GoHighLevel

Expand integration coverage to include additional contacts, deals, pipelines, custom fields, workflows as needed

Knows how to use Zapier, Make, Pabbly, or n8n to connect WordPress with CRM

Troubleshoot and resolve issues with current integrations

Custom WordPress Development

Build or customize themes using block-based, Full Site Editing, or classic theme approaches

Write backend logic in PHP using WordPress hooks, actions, filters, and the REST API

Create custom post types, taxonomies, meta boxes, and advanced field configurations



Requirements

2–3+ years of WordPress development experience (themes, plugins, and full-stack builds)

WooCommerce experience including store customization, custom extensions, and performance optimization

Understanding of UX design principles and how user behavior applies to web page layouts

Hands-on experience with AI coding tools such as Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, or equivalent

Experience working with Zoho CRM and/or GoHighLevel integrations (contacts, deals, pipelines, webhooks)

Working knowledge of at least one of: Zapier, Make, Pabbly, n8n

Proficiency in PHP, JavaScript, HTML.

Comfortable inheriting and improving existing codebases and integrations



What We Offer

Flexible schedule — work when you're most creative, no time tracker, no micromanagement

Performance-based salary growth — Strong performers move up fast

A collaborative team that values creative input and fresh ideas

Access to AI tools and a professional development budget

Competitive compensation based on experience

A supportive team environment where asking questions is encouraged

Manageable workload with clear priorities — we focus on one thing at a time, not everything at once

Hands-on guidance and clear direction from the team — you will not be left to figure everything out alone

Ready to Apply?



I'd love to see what you can create! To help me understand if we're a great fit, please share the following:



1. Your DISC test - Take it here - 
/disc-personality-test/ - Please send a link to your results.

2. Your personality test - Take it here - 
/free-personality-test - Please send a link to your results.

3. Your updated resume, showcasing your most relevant experience.

4. Typing Test - Take this typing test (30 seconds):


p?minutes=0.5&textfile=0&mode=sent&result_url=
p&bt=0&gt=normal



5. A video or videos that answer the following. Feel free to give as much information and context as possible. Also please make sure that, when you submit the video(s) links, they are in public, viewable format.



a. Tell us about yourself. Hobbies/passions. What are your career/life goals and in what amount of time?

b. Show off your work setup (computer/laptop, monitor(s), speakers, lights, etc) and tech setup - browser you use, other tech/platforms you use

c. Think of one objective you’ve had to complete in your current or previous role. Explain what metric(s) were you measured on (time spent, money saved, traffic/engagement/leads/sales, etc). Reverse engineer said objective with the target measurement in mind. Map out the process that you came up with in google docs, google sheets, or in Canva as a visual representation. How could you have made it easier/faster/better using AI?



I'm looking forward to seeing your work and learning more about you. If you have any questions before applying, feel free to reach out!

title: Zoho CRM Administrator (Deluge & Integrations)
description:
Overview

We are seeking an experienced Zoho CRM Administrator with strong Deluge scripting and integration expertise to manage, customize, and optimize our Zoho CRM and Zoho One environment. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience building custom automations, API integrations, and webhooks, particularly integrating Zoho CRM with WordPress-based systems and external platforms.

This role involves designing scalable workflows, writing custom Deluge code, and implementing API-based integrations to streamline business processes.



About Us:

We are a luxury watch–focused brand seeking to enhance our content and e-commerce strategy. Our audience values accurate, detailed, and refined information on high-end timepieces, including Rolex, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet, Omega, and other leading brands.



Key Responsibilities

Zoho CRM Administration

Manage and maintain Zoho CRM and Zoho One applications

Configure modules, fields, layouts, workflows, and automation

Monitor CRM data quality and system performance

Manage user roles, permissions, and access controls

Implement best practices for CRM architecture and data structure

Deluge Development

Develop custom Deluge scripts to extend CRM functionality

Create and maintain custom functions, workflows, and automation

Implement complex logic for business processes

Build custom configurations across Zoho One apps

API & Integration Development

Design and implement API integrations between Zoho CRM and external systems

Manage webhooks to and from our WordPress website

Integrate Zoho CRM with third-party tools and services

Troubleshoot and maintain integration reliability

Ensure secure and efficient data exchange between systems

WordPress Integration

Connect Zoho CRM with WordPress forms, plugins, and backend systems

Configure webhook endpoints and API calls

Maintain and optimize data synchronization between WordPress and Zoho

System Optimization & Support

Identify opportunities to improve workflows and automations

Debug errors in Deluge scripts and integrations

Maintain documentation for CRM configurations and integrations

Support internal teams with CRM improvements and training



Required Skills & Qualifications

Technical Skills

Strong experience with Zoho CRM administration

Advanced knowledge of Deluge scripting

Experience with Zoho One ecosystem

Solid understanding of REST APIs

Experience implementing webhooks

Experience integrating Zoho CRM with WordPress

Knowledge of JSON, HTTP requests, and API authentication

Experience building custom automation and workflows

Preferred Skills

Experience with Zoho Flow or other integration platforms

Familiarity with WordPress plugins and backend integrations

Experience integrating CRM systems with marketing, payment, or SaaS tools

Understanding of data structures and system architecture



Experience Requirements

Proven experience writing Deluge scripts

Demonstrated experience building API integrations and webhooks

Experience integrating CRM systems with WordPress



Nice to Have

Experience with Zoho Creator

Experience with automation and workflow optimization

Knowledge of CRM data analytics and reporting



Key Success Metrics

Reliable and scalable CRM automation

Stable integrations between Zoho CRM and external systems

Reduced manual processes through automation

Improved data accuracy and workflow efficiency



Work Setup

Full time remote position

Flexible working hours

No time tracker. We focus on results and deliverables

Collaborative, asynchronous communication environment

 Your DISC test -

title: Paid Ads and Creative Strategist
description:
About the Role



We are looking for a Paid Ads & Creative Strategist who understands both performance marketing and creative strategy. This role is ideal for someone who can not only manage paid advertising campaigns but also think strategically about the creative angles, messaging, and ad concepts that drive conversions.



You will work closely with our team to plan, launch, optimize, and scale paid advertising campaigns, while continuously testing new creative ideas to improve performance.

 & Instagram), Google Ads, and other channels

• Proactive – constantly testing new ideas and strategies

• Reliable and organized – able to manage campaigns independently

title: Hiring: Video Editing VA for Vlog Content, Shorts, and Thumbnails
description:
Hiring: Video Editing VA for Vlog Content, Shorts, and Thumbnails

I’m looking for a Video Editing VA to help manage a weekly content pipeline centered around vlog-style videos.

We produce 4 YouTube videos per week, each around 5–7 minutes long, and need someone who can primarily clip long-form content into short-form, create high-quality thumbnails, and handle minor editing on the vlog videos themselves.

This is not a heavy cinematic editing role. The content is more personality-driven and natural, so the main skill is being able to identify strong 
nts, shape them into engaging short-form clips, and make the content feel native to each platform.

Responsibilities:

Create high-quality YouTube thumbnails





Clip each weekly YouTube video into short-form content for Instagram Reels and TikTok





Produce separate YouTube Shorts based on direction/material provided by me





Make minor edits to vlog-style long-form videos





Apply feedback and revise edits as needed





Weekly workflow:

I review all 4 YouTube videos for the week





I rank them from strongest to weakest and give feedback





You revise accordingly, while also working on the next batch of content





This will likely be an ongoing, near full-time role for the right person.

To apply, send:

Your portfolio





Examples of vlog-style edits





Examples of short-form clips made from long-form content





Thumbnail samples



I'm looking for someone who can edit a vlog like: 



I want to specifically see people who are good at vlog content, not just highly polished commercial edits.



Please start your application with the phrase: Video Editor



This helps us know you read the full post carefully.

Supportive team environment



Opportunity for growth into senior or leadership roles

Ongoing training and development



Final Note

We are looking for someone who already knows the job and can deliver consistently.

title: Virtual Patient Care Coordinator
description:
We are seeking a highly professional, confident, and tech-savvy Virtual Patient Care Coordinator to serve as the virtual front desk and operational anchor of the clinic.



This is not a basic administrative role—you will be the voice, presence, and flow manager of the clinic during treatment hours while also supporting backend systems and patient engagement outside of clinic hours. Your communication skills, organization, and composure will directly impact both the patient experience and clinic growth.



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants to grow with a mission-driven healthcare practice.



Your Role

You will operate as the clinic’s virtual front desk during live treatment hours, ensuring seamless communication, accurate documentation, and consistent patient follow-up.



You must be confident on the phone, able to guide conversations toward booking consultations, and maintain professionalism and authority without discussing pricing or insurance details.



Core Responsibilities



Live Clinic Support (Real-Time)

* Join live during clinic hours via FaceTime or designated platform

* Answer incoming calls with warmth, confidence, and professionalism

* Guide prospective patients toward booking consultations

* Monitor reception camera feed to track patient arrivals and flow

* Support virtual patient check-in process

* Collect patient updates and log into workflow tracker (Google Sheets)

* Transfer patient information into EHR (OptiMantra) using templates

* Generate superbills and create invoices

* Track visit packages and session usage

* Prepare and send Good Faith Estimates

* Communicate with patients via phone,
, and Google Voice



Patient Follow-Up & Retention

* Follow up with consultation leads who did not convert

* Re-engage patients who have paused or dropped off care

* Conduct outreach campaigns during designated periods

* Maintain rapport and continuity with active patients



Administrative & Systems Support

* Maintain accurate and organized electronic health records (OptiMantra)

* Track daily patient workflow using Google Workspace

* Support light billing (invoices, payment plans, superbills)

* Ensure documentation accuracy and completeness

* Maintain HIPAA compliance (training provided)

* Keep systems, files, and processes organized and up to date



Off-Hour / Flexible Tasks

* Return missed calls (30–60 minutes daily as needed)

* Schedule and post social media content (Canva + OneUp)

* Make light edits to pre-developed content

* Assist with additional admin or marketing initiatives as the clinic grows



Tools & Platforms Requires

Hubstaff, Google Workspace (Drive, Sheets, Voice), iCloud, Eufy Security, Tuya, Alexa, Plaud, Canon Print, Canva, OneUp, Zoom



Skills & Qualifications



Required

* Near-native English fluency with a clear, neutral accent

* Confident, professional, and warm phone presence

* Strong communication and rapport-building skills

* Ability to guide conversations and handle objections

* Highly organized, detail-oriented, and dependable

* Tech-savvy and comfortable managing multiple systems simultaneously

* Proficient with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive)

* Comfortable learning and using EHR systems (OptiMantra experience a plus)

* Ability to work independently without constant supervision

* Reliable high-speed internet (backup connection preferred)



Preferred (Nice-to-Have)

* Experience in healthcare, wellness, acupuncture, or medical office settings

* Experience with OptiMantra or other EHR systems

* Background in patient retention or client success

* Experience with virtual front desk operations

* Familiarity with Canva and social media scheduling tools

* Access to Apple device (iMessage/FaceTime) for clinic communication



What We’re Looking For

You are:

* Warm, empathetic, and patient-centered

* Confident and composed under pressure

* Organized and systems-oriented

* Coachable and open to feedback

* Professional with a strong sense of ownership

* Looking for a long-term role, not a temporary position



How to Apply

Please submit your application along with a brief video introduction.



Applications without a video will not be considered
